older thoughts on sensitivity plots

maybe instead of looking at the strategy score, plot the outplanting proportions that maximize each performance metric (with different colors for the different metrics)? Or the main metrics of interest since there's so much correlation: 1) coral cover on reef, 2) orchard function, 3) reef ROI, and 4) total ROI. Can put them all on the same plot since it's just the proportion that maximizes each. But also want to show the actual values...and the shape of the relationship...


# orchard transplanting

try some orchard transplanting scenarios

```{r}

years <- 51 # number of years in simulation
n <- 5 # number of size classes

# define size class boundaries
SC1 <- 0
SC2 <- 10
SC3 <- 100
SC4 <- 900
SC5 <- 4000

A_mids <- c((SC1 + SC2)/2, (SC2 + SC3)/2, (SC3 + SC4)/2, (SC4 + SC5)/2, 9325) # midpoint areas of each size class (cm^2); last value is the 50% quantile of observations greater than SC5 in the standardized data from the literature

A_reef <- mean(c(4492.011, 10076.67, 8943.55)) # area of the reef (very rough estimates from kml file of Fundemar's sites)

# set upt the orchard, reef, and lab treatments
orchard_treatments <- c("orchard1") # orchards (could have multiple orchards and/or orchards with different post-outplanting treatments)
reef_treatments <- c("reef1") # reef treatments/subpopulations (e.g., could have "urchin outplanting" or "algal removal" or other postoutplanting treatments)
lab_treatments <- c("0_T1", "0_T2") # lab treatments (here there are just two to keep corals on the reef that originated in the orchard separate from those that came straight from the lab)


# demographic parameters for each orchard and reef
field_surv1 <- field_surv
field_growth1 <- field_growth
nurs_surv1 <- nurs_surv
nurs_growth1 <- nurs_growth

# nursery data only available for size classes 1-2, so use the field data for the larger size classes
nurs_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df <- nurs_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df %>% filter(size_class < 3) %>% rbind(field_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df[field_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df$size_class > 2,])

nurs_growth1$summ_list[[3]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[3]]
nurs_growth1$summ_list[[4]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[4]]
nurs_growth1$summ_list[[5]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[5]]

# calculate mean parameter values (survival, growth, shrinkage, fragmentation) for each 
all_pars <- default_pars_fun(n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments), summ_metric_list = list(field_surv = "mean", field_growth = "mean", field_shrink = "mean", field_frag = "mean", nurs_surv = "mean", nurs_growth = "mean", nurs_shrink = "mean"), field_surv1, field_growth1, nurs_surv1, nurs_growth1, apal_frag_summ)

# reef parameters: survival
surv_pars.r <- all_pars$surv_pars.r

# reef parameters: growth
growth_pars.r <- all_pars$growth_pars.r

# reef parameters: shrinkage
shrink_pars.r <- all_pars$shrink_pars.r

# reef parameters: fragmentation
frag_pars.r <- all_pars$frag_pars.r

# density dependent survival (smallest size class only for now)
dens_pars.r <- list()
dens_pars.r[[1]] <- list() # first reef treatment/subpop
dens_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- 0.02 # first source to first reef (external recruits)
dens_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- dens_pars.r[[1]][[1]] # second source to first reef (first lab treatment)
dens_pars.r[[1]][[3]] <- dens_pars.r[[1]][[1]] # third source to first reef (second lab treatment)

# reef parameters: fecundity (for tracking reef reproductive output)
# estimates of number of eggs from spawning colonies in Fundemar's data (but don't know colony sizes): min = ~5200, 25% quantile = ~30000, 50% = ~93000, 75% = ~100000, max = ~140000, mean = ~72000

# update: use Fundemar's 2025 data (table 1 of their annual report) and assume no size-dependent reproduction because 1) lack of data and 2) Fundemar said they sometimes see smaller colonies spawning the most
# from Table 1: collected 1255111 embryos (100% fertilization) from 26 colonies (26 out of 31 of their monitored colonies spawned over a period of 2 days)

fec_pars.r <- list()
fec_pars.r[[1]] <- list()
fec_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- c(0, 0, rep(1255111/26, 3)) #c(0, 0, 5000, 50000, 100000)
fec_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- fec_pars.r[[1]][[1]]
fec_pars.r[[1]][[3]] <- fec_pars.r[[1]][[1]]

# orchard parameters: survival
surv_pars.o <-all_pars$surv_pars.o #  list(list(c(0.8888889, 0.9314815, 0.95, 0.98, 1)))

# orchard parameters: growth
growth_pars.o <- all_pars$growth_pars.o

# orchard parameters: shrinkage
shrink_pars.o <- all_pars$shrink_pars.o

# orchard parameters: fragmentation
frag_pars.o <- all_pars$frag_pars.o

# density dependent survival (smallest size class only for now)
dens_pars.o <- list()
dens_pars.o[[1]] <- list() # first orchard
dens_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- 0.02
dens_pars.o[[1]][[2]] <- 0.02

# orchard parameters: fecundity
fec_pars.o <- list()
fec_pars.o[[1]] <- list() # first treatment
fec_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- c(0, 0, rep(1255111/26, 3)) # first source
fec_pars.o[[1]][[2]] <- c(0, 0, rep(1255111/26, 3)) # second source

lambda <- 0 # external recruitment to reef

#lambda_R <- 10000 # mean number of larvae produced by reference reefs each year
lambda_R <- 1255111 # from Table 1 of Fundemar's report (total embryos collected in 2025)

# stochasticity parameters
sigma_s <- 0
sigma_f <- 0
ext_rand <- c(FALSE, FALSE) # whether 1) external recruitment and 2) reference reef reproduction is stochastic 
seeds <- c(1000, 5000, 10000, 40000)

# initial conditions in each reef subpopulation
N0.r <- list()
N0.r[[1]] <- list() # second reef subpop
N0.r[[1]][[1]] <- rep(0, n) # first source (external recruits)
N0.r[[1]][[2]] <- rep(0, n) # second source
N0.r[[1]][[3]] <- rep(0, n) # third source

# initial conditions in each orchard subpopulation
N0.o <- list()
N0.o[[1]] <- list() # first orchard treatment
N0.o[[1]][[1]] <- rep(0, n) # first source
N0.o[[1]][[2]] <- rep(0, n) # second source

N0.l <- list()
N0.l[[1]] <- 0
N0.l[[2]] <- 0


# disturbance parameters
dist_yrs <- c(years + 10) # years when disturbance occurs, can set to > years for none
#dist_yrs <- c(10)


# effects of disturbance on each reef subpop
dist_effects.r <- list()
dist_effects.r[[1]] <- list() # list where each element is the effects of disturbance on corals from each source in the 1st reef subpop (either "survival" for survival, "Tmat" for growth/survival, "Fmat" for fragmentation, or "fecundity" for reproduction)
dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]] <- list() # effects of disturbances on corals from first source in first reef subpop
dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]][[1]] <- c("survival") # effects of 1st disturbance on corals from first source in first reef subpop ([[1]][[1]][[2]] would be second disturbance, etc.)
dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]] <- list() # second source
dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]][[1]] <- c("survival")
dist_effects.r[[1]][[3]] <- list() # third source
dist_effects.r[[1]][[3]][[1]] <- c("survival")


# disturbance parameters for each reef subpop (what happens to the population parameters at each disturbance eve)
dist_pars.r <- list()

# first reef population
dist_pars.r[[1]] <- list() 
# first source to first reef subpop
dist_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.r[[1]][[1]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL) # list of disturbance parameters for the first source to the first reef population (defaults for each parameter type are NULL unless the disturbance affects them, as specified in dist_effects)
# second source to first reef subpop (lab tiles)
dist_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.r[[1]][[2]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL)
dist_pars.r[[1]][[3]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.r[[1]][[3]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.r[[1]][[3]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL)


# disturbance effects for each orchard subpop
dist_effects.o <- list()

dist_effects.o[[1]] <- list() 
dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]] <- list() # effects of disturbances on corals from first source in first orchard treatment
dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]][[1]] <- c("survival") # effects of 1st disturbance on corals from first source in first reef subpop ([[1]][[1]][[2]] would be second disturbance, etc.)

dist_effects.o[[1]][[2]] <- list() # effects of disturbances on corals from second source in first orchard treatment
dist_effects.o[[1]][[2]][[1]] <- c("survival") # effects of 1st disturbance on corals from first source in first reef subpop ([[1]][[1]][[2]] would be second disturbance, etc.)


# disturbance parameters for each orchard subpop
dist_pars.o <- list()

# first orchard treatment
dist_pars.o[[1]] <- list() 
# first source in first orchard treatment
dist_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.o[[1]][[1]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL) 
# second source in first orchard
dist_pars.o[[1]][[2]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.o[[1]][[2]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.o[[1]][[2]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL) 



# lab parameters
# fractions of settled larvae in each lab treatment that survive to immediate outplanting each year
s0 <- matrix(NA, nrow = years, ncol = 2)
s0[,1] <- rep(0.95, years)
s0[,2] <- rep(0.95, years)

#s0[10,1] <- 0.05
#s0[10,2] <- 0.05

# fractions of settled larvae in each lab treatment that survive to 1 yr outplanting
s1 <- matrix(NA, nrow = years, ncol = 2)
s1[,1] <- rep(0.7, years)
s1[,2] <- rep(0.7, years)

m0 <- c(0.02, 0.02) # density dependent mortality rate of larvae in each lab treatment that get outplanted immediately
m1 <- c(0.02, 0.02) # density dependent mortality rate of larvae in each lab treatment that get outplanted after 1 year
#sett_props <- list(T1 = 0.15, T2 = 0.15) # proportion of larvae that settle on each tile type
sett_props <- list(T1 = 0.15, T2 = 0.15) # proportion of larvae that settle on each tile type
size_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = n) # matrix for fractions of retained recruits in each size class at the end of their year in the lab
size_props[1, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # first lab treatment (0_T1)
size_props[2, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # second lab treatment (0_T2)
size_props1 <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = n) # matrix for fractions of retained recruits in each size class at the end of their year in the lab
size_props1[1, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # first lab treatment (0_T1)
size_props1[2, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # second lab treatment (0_T2)
lab_pars <- list(s0 = s0, s1 = s1, m0 = m0, m1 = m1, sett_props = sett_props, size_props = size_props, size_props1 = size_props1)

# restoration strategy parameters
tile_props <- list(T1 = 0.5, T2 = 0.5) # proportion of tiles that are each type
orchard_yield <- 1 # percent of new orchard babies successfully collected
reef_yield <- 1 # percent of new reef babies successfully collected and fertilized
spawn_target <- 2000000 # target number of embryos to collect from orchard each year (note: if this is set to zero, there won't be any settlers)
reef_prop <- c(0.9, 0) # proportion of lab recruits from each lab treatment outplanted to reef (1-proportion outplanted to orchard)
reef_out_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = length(reef_treatments)) # proportion recruits going from each lab treatment to each reef treatment (row = origin lab treatment, column = destination reef treatment)
reef_out_props[1,] <- c(1) # from first lab treatment to each reef treatment (here there's just on intervention reef so they all go there)
reef_out_props[2,] <- c(1) # from second lab treatment to each reef treatment 

orchard_out_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = length(orchard_treatments)) # proportion recruits going from each lab treatment to each orchard treatment
orchard_out_props[1,] <- c(1) # from first lab treatment to each orchard 
orchard_out_props[2,] <- c(1) # from second lab treatment to each orchard 

# sizes allocated to each treatment
reef_areas <- c(A_reef)*10000 # m^2 given to each post-outplanting reef treatment/subpopulation, convert to cm^2 by multiplying by 10,000
lab_max <- 3100 # total number of tiles that the lab can accommodate 
lab_retain_max <- 0 # total number of tiles that the lab can keep for a year (must be less than or equal to lab_max; if > 0 then must have at least one "1_" lab treatment, if equal to lab max then must have all treatments be "1_")
tank_min <- 14600 # min number of embryos to put in a tank (reduce number of tanks used if less than this to avoid unrealistically small densities on the tiles)
tank_max <- 33333 # max number of embryos to put in a tank (impose a max because in reality if you had unlimited embryos you wouldn't put all of them in tanks because the density would be too high and presumably lead to high dens dep mortality). Chose max to correspond to number that would result in 50 embryos per tile, assuming 15% settlement and 100 tiles per tank

# 100*50/0.15

orchard_size <- c(30*500) # number of tiles each orchard has space for (500 stars x 30 tiles per star)


# coral transplanting
transplant <- rep(0, years) # years in which corals get transplanted from the orchard
#transplant[10] <- 1 # in 10th year, move corals from orchard to reef

null_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = years, ncol = n)
#rest_pars$trans_mats[[1]][[1]][which(transplant!=0)[1],] <- c(0, 0, 0, 2,0)# max number of colonies of each size class to move to reef in first transplant event
trans_mats <- list()
trans_mats[[1]] <- list() # first orchard 
trans_mats[[1]][[1]] <- null_mat # number of corals in each size class that originated from the first source of colonies for this orchard to transplant at each timepoint
trans_mats[[1]][[2]] <- null_mat # number of corals in each size class that originated from the second source of colonies for this orchard to transplant at each timepoint

# where on the reef to put the transplants
#rest_pars$trans_reef[[1]][[1]][which(transplant!=0)[1],] <- c(1, 1)# reef and source subpop to transport the corals to
trans_reef <- list()
trans_reef[[1]] <- list() # first orchard
trans_reef[[1]][[1]] <- matrix(c(1, 2), nrow = years, ncol = 2, byrow = T) # first source corals in this orchard get transplanted to the second source of corals in the first reef
trans_reef[[1]][[2]] <- matrix(c(1, 3), nrow = years, ncol = 2, byrow = T) # second source corals in this orchard get transplanted to the third source of corals in the second reef

rest_pars <- list(tile_props = tile_props, orchard_yield = orchard_yield, reef_yield = reef_yield, spawn_target = spawn_target, reef_prop = reef_prop, reef_out_props = reef_out_props, orchard_out_props = orchard_out_props, reef_areas = reef_areas, lab_max = lab_max, lab_retain_max = lab_retain_max, tank_min = tank_min, tank_max = tank_max, orchard_size = orchard_size, transplant = transplant, trans_mats = trans_mats, trans_reef = trans_reef, trans_surv = trans_surv)


sim1 <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars, rest_pars, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)

```


```{r}
# check results

# plot each reef population separately
plot(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$reef_pops_pre[[1]][[1]], MARGIN = 2, sum), type = "l", las = 1, ylim = c(0, 1000), xlab = "year", ylab = "Pop'n size", lwd = 2)
lines(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$reef_pops_pre[[1]][[2]], MARGIN = 2, sum), lty = 2)
lines(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$reef_pops_pre[[1]][[3]], MARGIN = 2, sum), lty = 3)

# sim1$reef_pops_pre[[1]][[1]][c(3:5),]

plot(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$orchard_pops_pre[[1]][[1]], MARGIN = 2, sum), type = "l", las = 1, ylim = c(0, 60000), xlab = "year", ylab = "Pop'n size", lwd = 2)
lines(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$orchard_pops_pre[[1]][[2]], MARGIN = 2, sum), lty = 2)

# just the transplanted orchard population
plot(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$orchard_pops_pre[[1]][[2]], MARGIN = 2, sum), type = "l", las = 1, ylim = c(0, 40000), xlab = "year", ylab = "Pop'n size", lwd = 2)

```



repeat with just one tile type

```{r}

years <- 51 # number of years in simulation
n <- 5 # number of size classes


SC1 <- 0
SC2 <- 10
SC3 <- 100
SC4 <- 900
SC5 <- 4000

A_mids <- c((SC1 + SC2)/2, (SC2 + SC3)/2, (SC3 + SC4)/2, (SC4 + SC5)/2, 9325) # midpoint areas of each size class (cm^2); last value is the 50% quantile of observations greater than SC5 in the standardized data from the literature

A_reef <- mean(c(4492.011, 10076.67, 8943.55)) # area of the reef (very rough estimates from kml file of Fundemar's sites)

# set upt the orchard, reef, and lab treatments
orchard_treatments <- c("orchard1") 
reef_treatments <- c("reef1") 
lab_treatments <- c("0_T1") 

# demographic parameters for each orchard and reef
field_surv1 <- field_surv
field_growth1 <- field_growth
nurs_surv1 <- nurs_surv
nurs_growth1 <- nurs_growth

# nursery data only available for size classes 1-2, so use the field data for the larger size classes
nurs_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df <- nurs_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df %>% filter(size_class < 3) %>% rbind(field_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df[field_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df$size_class > 2,])

nurs_growth1$summ_list[[3]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[3]]
nurs_growth1$summ_list[[4]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[4]]
nurs_growth1$summ_list[[5]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[5]]

# calculate mean parameter values (survival, growth, shrinkage, fragmentation) for each 
all_pars <- default_pars_fun(n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments), summ_metric_list = list(field_surv = "mean", field_growth = "mean", field_shrink = "mean", field_frag = "mean", nurs_surv = "mean", nurs_growth = "mean", nurs_shrink = "mean"), field_surv1, field_growth1, nurs_surv1, nurs_growth1, apal_frag_summ)

# reef parameters: survival
surv_pars.r <- all_pars$surv_pars.r

# reef parameters: growth
growth_pars.r <- all_pars$growth_pars.r

# reef parameters: shrinkage
shrink_pars.r <- all_pars$shrink_pars.r

# reef parameters: fragmentation
frag_pars.r <- all_pars$frag_pars.r

# density dependent survival (smallest size class only for now)
dens_pars.r <- list()
dens_pars.r[[1]] <- list() # first reef treatment/subpop
dens_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- 0.02 # first source to first reef (external recruits)
dens_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- dens_pars.r[[1]][[1]] # second source to first reef (first lab treatment)


fec_pars.r <- list()
fec_pars.r[[1]] <- list()
fec_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- c(0, 0, rep(1255111/26, 3)) #c(0, 0, 5000, 50000, 100000)
fec_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- fec_pars.r[[1]][[1]]

# orchard parameters: survival
surv_pars.o <-all_pars$surv_pars.o #  list(list(c(0.8888889, 0.9314815, 0.95, 0.98, 1)))

# orchard parameters: growth
growth_pars.o <- all_pars$growth_pars.o

# orchard parameters: shrinkage
shrink_pars.o <- all_pars$shrink_pars.o

# orchard parameters: fragmentation
frag_pars.o <- all_pars$frag_pars.o

# density dependent survival (smallest size class only for now)
dens_pars.o <- list()
dens_pars.o[[1]] <- list() # first orchard
dens_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- 0.02

# orchard parameters: fecundity
fec_pars.o <- list()
fec_pars.o[[1]] <- list() # first treatment
fec_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- c(0, 0, rep(1255111/26, 3)) # first source

lambda <- 0 # external recruitment to reef

#lambda_R <- 10000 # mean number of larvae produced by reference reefs each year
lambda_R <- 1255111 # from Table 1 of Fundemar's report (total embryos collected in 2025)

# stochasticity parameters
sigma_s <- 0
sigma_f <- 0
ext_rand <- c(FALSE, FALSE) # whether 1) external recruitment and 2) reference reef reproduction is stochastic 
seeds <- c(1000, 5000, 10000, 40000)



# disturbance parameters
dist_yrs <- c(years + 10) # years when disturbance occurs, can set to > years for none
#dist_yrs <- c(10)


# effects of disturbance on each reef subpop
dist_effects.r <- list()
dist_effects.r[[1]] <- list() # list where each element is the effects of disturbance on corals from each source in the 1st reef subpop (either "survival" for survival, "Tmat" for growth/survival, "Fmat" for fragmentation, or "fecundity" for reproduction)
dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]] <- list() # effects of disturbances on corals from first source in first reef subpop
dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]][[1]] <- c("survival") # effects of 1st disturbance on corals from first source in first reef subpop ([[1]][[1]][[2]] would be second disturbance, etc.)
dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]] <- list() # second source
dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]][[1]] <- c("survival")


# disturbance parameters for each reef subpop (what happens to the population parameters at each disturbance eve)
dist_pars.r <- list()

# first reef population
dist_pars.r[[1]] <- list() 
# first source to first reef subpop
dist_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.r[[1]][[1]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL) # list of disturbance parameters for the first source to the first reef population (defaults for each parameter type are NULL unless the disturbance affects them, as specified in dist_effects)
# second source to first reef subpop (lab tiles)
dist_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.r[[1]][[2]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL)


# disturbance effects for each orchard subpop
dist_effects.o <- list()

dist_effects.o[[1]] <- list() 
dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]] <- list() # effects of disturbances on corals from first source in first orchard treatment
dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]][[1]] <- c("survival") # effects of 1st disturbance on corals from first source in first reef subpop ([[1]][[1]][[2]] would be second disturbance, etc.)

# disturbance parameters for each orchard subpop
dist_pars.o <- list()

# first orchard treatment
dist_pars.o[[1]] <- list() 
# first source in first orchard treatment
dist_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.o[[1]][[1]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL) 

# lab parameters
# fractions of settled larvae in each lab treatment that survive to immediate outplanting each year
s0 <- matrix(NA, nrow = years, ncol = 2)
s0[,1] <- rep(0.95, years)
s0[,2] <- rep(0.95, years)

#s0[10,1] <- 0.05
#s0[10,2] <- 0.05

# fractions of settled larvae in each lab treatment that survive to 1 yr outplanting
s1 <- matrix(NA, nrow = years, ncol = 2)
s1[,1] <- rep(0.7, years)
s1[,2] <- rep(0.7, years)

m0 <- c(0.02, 0.02) # density dependent mortality rate of larvae in each lab treatment that get outplanted immediately
m1 <- c(0.02, 0.02) # density dependent mortality rate of larvae in each lab treatment that get outplanted after 1 year
#sett_props <- list(T1 = 0.15, T2 = 0.15) # proportion of larvae that settle on each tile type
sett_props <- list(T1 = 0.15) # proportion of larvae that settle on each tile type
size_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = n) # matrix for fractions of recruits in each size class at time of outplanting
size_props[1, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # first lab treatment (0_T1)
#size_props[2, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # second lab treatment (0_T2)
size_props1 <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = n) # matrix for fractions of retained recruits in each size class at time of outplanting
size_props1[1, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # first lab treatment (0_T1)
lab_pars <- list(s0 = s0, s1 = s1, m0 = m0, m1 = m1, sett_props = sett_props, size_props = size_props, size_props1 = size_props1)

# restoration strategy parameters
tile_props <- list(T1 = 1) # proportion of tiles that are each type
orchard_yield <- 1 # percent of new orchard babies successfully collected
reef_yield <- 1 # percent of new reef babies successfully collected and fertilized
spawn_target <- 2000000 # target number of embryos to collect from orchard each year (note: if this is set to zero, there won't be any settlers)
reef_prop <- c(0.9) # proportion of lab recruits from each lab treatment outplanted to reef (1-proportion outplanted to orchard)
reef_out_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = length(reef_treatments)) # proportion recruits going from each lab treatment to each reef treatment (row = origin lab treatment, column = destination reef treatment)
reef_out_props[1,] <- c(1) # from first lab treatment to each reef treatment (here there's just on intervention reef so they all go there)

orchard_out_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = length(orchard_treatments)) # proportion recruits going from each lab treatment to each orchard treatment
orchard_out_props[1,] <- c(1) # from first lab treatment to each orchard 

# sizes allocated to each treatment
reef_areas <- c(A_reef)*10000 # m^2 given to each post-outplanting reef treatment/subpopulation, convert to cm^2 by multiplying by 10,000
lab_max <- 3100 # total number of tiles that the lab can accommodate 
lab_retain_max <- 0 # total number of tiles that the lab can keep for a year (must be less than or equal to lab_max; if > 0 then must have at least one "1_" lab treatment, if equal to lab max then must have all treatments be "1_")
tank_min <- 14600 # min number of embryos to put in a tank (reduce number of tanks used if less than this to avoid unrealistically small densities on the tiles)
tank_max <- 33333 # max number of embryos to put in a tank (impose a max because in reality if you had unlimited embryos you wouldn't put all of them in tanks because the density would be too high and presumably lead to high dens dep mortality). Chose max to correspond to number that would result in 50 embryos per tile, assuming 15% settlement and 100 tiles per tank

# 100*50/0.15

orchard_size <- c(30*500) # number of tiles each orchard has space for (500 stars x 30 tiles per star)



# initial conditions in each reef subpopulation
N0.r <- list()
N0.r[[1]] <- list() # second reef subpop
N0.r[[1]][[1]] <- rep(0, n) # first source (external recruits)
N0.r[[1]][[2]] <- rep(0, n) # second source

# initial conditions in each orchard subpopulation
N0.o <- list()
N0.o[[1]] <- list() # first orchard treatment
N0.o[[1]][[1]] <- c(0, 0, 30*500/3,30*500/3, 30*500/3) # start with a mature orchard (should make this more precise with model output though)

N0.l <- list()
N0.l[[1]] <- 0
N0.l[[2]] <- 0

# coral transplanting
transplant <- rep(1, years) # years in which corals get transplanted from the orchard
#transplant[10] <- 1 # in 10th year, move corals from orchard to reef

null_mat <- matrix(orchard_size*0.1, nrow = years, ncol = n)
#rest_pars$trans_mats[[1]][[1]][which(transplant!=0)[1],] <- c(0, 0, 0, 2,0)# max number of colonies of each size class to move to reef in first transplant event
trans_mats <- list()
trans_mats[[1]] <- list() # first orchard 
trans_mats[[1]][[1]] <- null_mat # number of corals in each size class that originated from the first source of colonies for this orchard to transplant at each timepoint

# where on the reef to put the transplants
#rest_pars$trans_reef[[1]][[1]][which(transplant!=0)[1],] <- c(1, 1)# reef and source subpop to transport the corals to
trans_reef <- list()
trans_reef[[1]] <- list() # first orchard
trans_reef[[1]][[1]] <- matrix(c(1, 2), nrow = years, ncol = 2, byrow = T) # first source corals in this orchard get transplanted to the second source of corals in the first reef

rest_pars <- list(tile_props = tile_props, orchard_yield = orchard_yield, reef_yield = reef_yield, spawn_target = spawn_target, reef_prop = reef_prop, reef_out_props = reef_out_props, orchard_out_props = orchard_out_props, reef_areas = reef_areas, lab_max = lab_max, lab_retain_max = lab_retain_max, tank_min = tank_min, tank_max = tank_max, orchard_size = orchard_size, transplant = transplant, trans_mats = trans_mats, trans_reef = trans_reef, trans_surv = trans_surv)


sim1 <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars, rest_pars, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)

```


set transplant number higher than orchard capacity so all adults will be transplanted


```{r}
# check results

# plot each reef population separately
plot(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$reef_pops_pre[[1]][[1]], MARGIN = 2, sum), type = "l", las = 1, ylim = c(0, 10000), xlab = "year", ylab = "Pop'n size", lwd = 2)
lines(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$reef_pops_pre[[1]][[2]], MARGIN = 2, sum), lty = 2)


# sim1$reef_pops_pre[[1]][[1]][c(3:5),]

plot(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$orchard_pops_pre[[1]][[1]][c(3:5),], MARGIN = 2, sum), type = "l", las = 1, ylim = c(0, 60000), xlab = "year", ylab = "Orchard adults", lwd = 2)
abline(h = orchard_size, lty = 2)

plot(x = c(1:years), y = apply(sim1$orchard_pops_pre[[1]][[1]][c(1:2),], MARGIN = 2, sum), type = "l", las = 1, ylim = c(0, 60000), xlab = "year", ylab = "Orchard babies", lwd = 2)
abline(h = orchard_size, lty = 2)


plot(x = c(1:years), y = sim1$orchard_tiles[[1]], type = "l", las = 1, ylim = c(0, 60000), xlab = "year", ylab = "Pop'n size", lwd = 2)
abline(h = orchard_size, lty = 2)

```

# lab grow-out

## set up defaults

```{r}

years <- 51 # number of years in simulation
n <- 5 # number of size classes

# define size class boundaries
SC1 <- 0
SC2 <- 10
SC3 <- 100
SC4 <- 900
SC5 <- 4000

A_mids <- c((SC1 + SC2)/2, (SC2 + SC3)/2, (SC3 + SC4)/2, (SC4 + SC5)/2, 9325) # midpoint areas of each size class (cm^2); last value is the 50% quantile of observations greater than SC5 in the standardized data from the literature

A_reef <- mean(c(4492.011, 10076.67, 8943.55)) # area of the reef (very rough estimates from kml file of Fundemar's sites)

# set upt the orchard, reef, and lab treatments
orchard_treatments <- c("orchard1") # orchards (could have multiple orchards and/or orchards with different post-outplanting treatments)
reef_treatments <- c("reef1") # reef treatments/subpopulations (e.g., could have "urchin outplanting" or "algal removal" or other postoutplanting treatments)
lab_treatments <- c("0_T1", "1_T1") # lab treatments (cement = T1, ceramic = T2). "TX" is tile type, "X_" indicates whether recruits are outplanted immediately (0_) or the next year (0_1)


# demographic parameters for each orchard and reef
field_surv1 <- field_surv
field_growth1 <- field_growth
nurs_surv1 <- nurs_surv
nurs_growth1 <- nurs_growth

# nursery data only available for size classes 1-2, so use the field data for the larger size classes
nurs_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df <- nurs_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df %>% filter(size_class < 3) %>% rbind(field_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df[field_surv1$SC_surv_summ_df$size_class > 2,])

nurs_growth1$summ_list[[3]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[3]]
nurs_growth1$summ_list[[4]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[4]]
nurs_growth1$summ_list[[5]] <- field_growth1$summ_list[[5]]

# calculate mean parameter values (survival, growth, shrinkage, fragmentation) for each 
all_pars <- default_pars_fun(n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments), summ_metric_list = list(field_surv = "mean", field_growth = "mean", field_shrink = "mean", field_frag = "mean", nurs_surv = "mean", nurs_growth = "mean", nurs_shrink = "mean"), field_surv1, field_growth1, nurs_surv1, nurs_growth1, apal_frag_summ)

# reef parameters: survival
surv_pars.r <- all_pars$surv_pars.r

# reef parameters: growth
growth_pars.r <- all_pars$growth_pars.r

# reef parameters: shrinkage
shrink_pars.r <- all_pars$shrink_pars.r

# reef parameters: fragmentation
frag_pars.r <- all_pars$frag_pars.r

# density dependent survival (smallest size class only for now)
dens_pars.r <- list()
dens_pars.r[[1]] <- list() # first reef treatment/subpop
dens_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- 0.02 # first source to first reef (external recruits)
dens_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- dens_pars.r[[1]][[1]] # second source to first reef (first lab treatment)
dens_pars.r[[1]][[3]] <- dens_pars.r[[1]][[1]] # third source to first reef (second lab treatment)

# reef parameters: fecundity (for tracking reef reproductive output)
# estimates of number of eggs from spawning colonies in Fundemar's data (but don't know colony sizes): min = ~5200, 25% quantile = ~30000, 50% = ~93000, 75% = ~100000, max = ~140000, mean = ~72000

# update: use Fundemar's 2025 data (table 1 of their annual report) and assume no size-dependent reproduction because 1) lack of data and 2) Fundemar said they sometimes see smaller colonies spawning the most
# from Table 1: collected 1255111 embryos (100% fertilization) from 26 colonies (26 out of 31 of their monitored colonies spawned over a period of 2 days)

fec_pars.r <- list()
fec_pars.r[[1]] <- list()
fec_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- c(0, 0, rep(1255111/26, 3)) #c(0, 0, 5000, 50000, 100000)
fec_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- fec_pars.r[[1]][[1]]
fec_pars.r[[1]][[3]] <- fec_pars.r[[1]][[1]]

# orchard parameters: survival
surv_pars.o <-all_pars$surv_pars.o #  list(list(c(0.8888889, 0.9314815, 0.95, 0.98, 1)))

# orchard parameters: growth
growth_pars.o <- all_pars$growth_pars.o

# orchard parameters: shrinkage
shrink_pars.o <- all_pars$shrink_pars.o

# orchard parameters: fragmentation
frag_pars.o <- all_pars$frag_pars.o

# density dependent survival (smallest size class only for now)
dens_pars.o <- list()
dens_pars.o[[1]] <- list() # first orchard
dens_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- 0.02
dens_pars.o[[1]][[2]] <- 0.02

# orchard parameters: fecundity
fec_pars.o <- list()
fec_pars.o[[1]] <- list() # first treatment
fec_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- c(0, 0, rep(1255111/26, 3)) # first source
fec_pars.o[[1]][[2]] <- c(0, 0, rep(1255111/26, 3)) # second source

lambda <- 0 # external recruitment to reef

#lambda_R <- 10000 # mean number of larvae produced by reference reefs each year
lambda_R <- 1255111 # from Table 1 of Fundemar's report (total embryos collected in 2025)

# stochasticity parameters
sigma_s <- 0
sigma_f <- 0
ext_rand <- c(FALSE, FALSE) # whether 1) external recruitment and 2) reference reef reproduction is stochastic 
seeds <- c(1000, 5000, 10000, 40000)

# initial conditions in each reef subpopulation
N0.r <- list()
N0.r[[1]] <- list() # second reef subpop
N0.r[[1]][[1]] <- rep(0, n) # first source (external recruits)
N0.r[[1]][[2]] <- rep(0, n) # second source
N0.r[[1]][[3]] <- rep(0, n) # third source

# initial conditions in each orchard subpopulation
N0.o <- list()
N0.o[[1]] <- list() # first orchard treatment
N0.o[[1]][[1]] <- rep(0, n) # first source
N0.o[[1]][[2]] <- rep(0, n) # second source

N0.l <- list()
N0.l[[1]] <- 0
N0.l[[2]] <- 0


# disturbance parameters
dist_yrs <- c(years + 10) # years when disturbance occurs, can set to > years for none
#dist_yrs <- c(10)


# effects of disturbance on each reef subpop
dist_effects.r <- list()
dist_effects.r[[1]] <- list() # list where each element is the effects of disturbance on corals from each source in the 1st reef subpop (either "survival" for survival, "Tmat" for growth/survival, "Fmat" for fragmentation, or "fecundity" for reproduction)
dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]] <- list() # effects of disturbances on corals from first source in first reef subpop
dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]][[1]] <- c("survival") # effects of 1st disturbance on corals from first source in first reef subpop ([[1]][[1]][[2]] would be second disturbance, etc.)
dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]] <- list() # second source
dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]][[1]] <- c("survival")
dist_effects.r[[1]][[3]] <- list() # third source
dist_effects.r[[1]][[3]][[1]] <- c("survival")


# disturbance parameters for each reef subpop (what happens to the population parameters at each disturbance eve)
dist_pars.r <- list()

# first reef population
dist_pars.r[[1]] <- list() 
# first source to first reef subpop
dist_pars.r[[1]][[1]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.r[[1]][[1]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.r[[1]][[1]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL) # list of disturbance parameters for the first source to the first reef population (defaults for each parameter type are NULL unless the disturbance affects them, as specified in dist_effects)
# second source to first reef subpop (lab tiles)
dist_pars.r[[1]][[2]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.r[[1]][[2]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.r[[1]][[2]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL)
dist_pars.r[[1]][[3]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.r[[1]][[3]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.r[[1]][[3]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL)


# disturbance effects for each orchard subpop
dist_effects.o <- list()

dist_effects.o[[1]] <- list() 
dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]] <- list() # effects of disturbances on corals from first source in first orchard treatment
dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]][[1]] <- c("survival") # effects of 1st disturbance on corals from first source in first reef subpop ([[1]][[1]][[2]] would be second disturbance, etc.)

dist_effects.o[[1]][[2]] <- list() # effects of disturbances on corals from second source in first orchard treatment
dist_effects.o[[1]][[2]][[1]] <- c("survival") # effects of 1st disturbance on corals from first source in first reef subpop ([[1]][[1]][[2]] would be second disturbance, etc.)


# disturbance parameters for each orchard subpop
dist_pars.o <- list()

# first orchard treatment
dist_pars.o[[1]] <- list() 
# first source in first orchard treatment
dist_pars.o[[1]][[1]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.o[[1]][[1]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.o[[1]][[1]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL) 
# second source in first orchard
dist_pars.o[[1]][[2]] <- dist_pars_fun(dist_yrs = dist_yrs, dist_effects.o[[1]][[2]], dist_surv0 = list(surv_pars.o[[1]][[2]]*0.1), dist_Tmat0 = NULL, dist_Fmat0 = NULL, dist_fec0 = NULL) 



# lab parameters
# fractions of settled larvae in each lab treatment that survive to immediate outplanting each year
s0 <- matrix(NA, nrow = years, ncol = 2)
s0[,1] <- rep(0.95, years)
s0[,2] <- rep(0.95, years)

#s0[10,1] <- 0.05
#s0[10,2] <- 0.05

# fractions of settled larvae in each lab treatment that survive to 1 yr outplanting
s1 <- matrix(NA, nrow = years, ncol = 2)
s1[,1] <- rep(0.7, years)
s1[,2] <- rep(0.7, years)

m0 <- c(0.02, 0.02) # density dependent mortality rate of larvae in each lab treatment that get outplanted immediately
m1 <- c(0.02, 0.02) # density dependent mortality rate of larvae in each lab treatment that get outplanted after 1 year
#sett_props <- list(T1 = 0.15, T2 = 0.15) # proportion of larvae that settle on each tile type
sett_props <- list(T1 = 0.15) # proportion of larvae that settle on each tile type
size_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = n) # matrix for fractions of retained recruits in each size class at the end of their year in the lab
size_props[1, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # first lab treatment (0_T1)
#size_props[2, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # second lab treatment (0_T2)
size_props1 <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = n) # matrix for fractions of retained recruits in each size class at the end of their year in the lab
#size_props1[1, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # first lab treatment (0_T1)
size_props1[2, ] <- c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0) # second lab treatment (0_T2)
lab_pars <- list(s0 = s0, s1 = s1, m0 = m0, m1 = m1, sett_props = sett_props, size_props = size_props, size_props1 = size_props1)

# restoration strategy parameters
tile_props <- list(T1 = 1) # proportion of tiles that are each type
orchard_yield <- 1 # percent of new orchard babies successfully collected
reef_yield <- 1 # percent of new reef babies successfully collected and fertilized
spawn_target <- 2000000 # target number of embryos to collect from orchard each year (note: if this is set to zero, there won't be any settlers)
reef_prop <- c(1, 1) # proportion of lab recruits from each lab treatment outplanted to reef (1-proportion outplanted to orchard)
reef_out_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = length(reef_treatments)) # proportion recruits going from each lab treatment to each reef treatment (row = origin lab treatment, column = destination reef treatment)
reef_out_props[1,] <- c(1) # from first lab treatment to each reef treatment (here there's just on intervention reef so they all go there)
reef_out_props[2,] <- c(1) # from second lab treatment to each reef treatment 

orchard_out_props <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(lab_treatments), ncol = length(orchard_treatments)) # proportion recruits going from each lab treatment to each orchard treatment
orchard_out_props[1,] <- c(1) # from first lab treatment to each orchard 
orchard_out_props[2,] <- c(1) # from second lab treatment to each orchard 

# sizes allocated to each treatment
reef_areas <- c(A_reef)*10000 # m^2 given to each post-outplanting reef treatment/subpopulation, convert to cm^2 by multiplying by 10,000
lab_max <- 3100 # total number of tiles that the lab can accommodate 
lab_retain_max <- 0.5*3100 # total number of tiles that the lab can keep for a year (must be less than or equal to lab_max; if > 0 then must have at least one "1_" lab treatment, if equal to lab max then must have all treatments be "1_")
tank_min <- 14600 # min number of embryos to put in a tank (reduce number of tanks used if less than this to avoid unrealistically small densities on the tiles)
tank_max <- 33333 # max number of embryos to put in a tank (impose a max because in reality if you had unlimited embryos you wouldn't put all of them in tanks because the density would be too high and presumably lead to high dens dep mortality). Chose max to correspond to number that would result in 50 embryos per tile, assuming 15% settlement and 100 tiles per tank

# 100*50/0.15

orchard_size <- c(30*500) # number of tiles each orchard has space for (500 stars x 30 tiles per star)


# coral transplanting
transplant <- rep(0, years) # years in which corals get transplanted from the orchard
#transplant[10] <- 1 # in 10th year, move corals from orchard to reef

null_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = years, ncol = n)
#rest_pars$trans_mats[[1]][[1]][which(transplant!=0)[1],] <- c(0, 0, 0, 2,0)# max number of colonies of each size class to move to reef in first transplant event
trans_mats <- list()
trans_mats[[1]] <- list() # first orchard 
trans_mats[[1]][[1]] <- null_mat # number of corals in each size class that originated from the first source of colonies for this orchard to transplant at each timepoint
trans_mats[[1]][[2]] <- null_mat # number of corals in each size class that originated from the second source of colonies for this orchard to transplant at each timepoint

# where on the reef to put the transplants
#rest_pars$trans_reef[[1]][[1]][which(transplant!=0)[1],] <- c(1, 1)# reef and source subpop to transport the corals to
trans_reef <- list()
trans_reef[[1]] <- list() # first orchard
trans_reef[[1]][[1]] <- matrix(c(1, 2), nrow = years, ncol = 2, byrow = T) # first source corals in this orchard get transplanted to the second source of corals in the first reef
trans_reef[[1]][[2]] <- matrix(c(1, 3), nrow = years, ncol = 2, byrow = T) # second source corals in this orchard get transplanted to the third source of corals in the second reef

rest_pars <- list(tile_props = tile_props, orchard_yield = orchard_yield, reef_yield = reef_yield, spawn_target = spawn_target, reef_prop = reef_prop, reef_out_props = reef_out_props, orchard_out_props = orchard_out_props, reef_areas = reef_areas, lab_max = lab_max, lab_retain_max = lab_retain_max, tank_min = tank_min, tank_max = tank_max, orchard_size = orchard_size, transplant = transplant, trans_mats = trans_mats, trans_reef = trans_reef, trans_surv = trans_surv)


sim1 <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars, rest_pars, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)

```




start with simplest case: 5 year timeline, no orchard (because adding the orchard and how well they do in orchard vs. lab is another layer, and really just want to focus on maximizing survival on reef). And then look at 10 years or something to see if bet hedging takes longer to show up


maybe some sort of plot with relative survival of grow-out outplants (compared to those outplanted immediately) on the x-axis and relative growth on the y-axis? And then show optimal proportion of tiles to keep in lab?

```{r}
# update random parameters to have two lab treatments

# number of iterations
#n_sample1 <- 100

# full parameter sets
field_surv1 <- field_surv
field_growth1 <- field_growth
nurs_surv1 <- nurs_surv
nurs_growth1 <- nurs_growth

# nursery data only available for size classes 1-2, so use the field data for larger size classes
nurs_surv1$SC_surv_df <- nurs_surv1$SC_surv_df %>% filter(size_class < 3) %>% rbind(field_surv1$SC_surv_df[field_surv1$SC_surv_df$size_class > 2,])

nurs_growth1$mat_list[[3]] <- field_growth1$mat_list[[3]]
nurs_growth1$mat_list[[4]] <- field_growth1$mat_list[[4]]
nurs_growth1$mat_list[[5]] <- field_growth1$mat_list[[5]]

set.seed(500)
all_pars_R2 <- rand_pars_fun(n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments), n_sample = n_sample1, field_surv = field_surv1, field_growth = field_growth1, nurs_surv = nurs_surv1, nurs_growth = nurs_growth1, apal_frag)
```

## no disturbance

```{r}
# simulations for calculating baseline ROI values (no substrates retained)

rest_pars_ij <- rest_pars
rest_pars_ij$lab_retain_max <- 0

lab_grow_base <- list() # holding list for baseline values

for(i in 1:n_sample1){ # for each parameter set
  
  # set up the parameters
  
  surv_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$surv_pars_L.r[[i]] 
  growth_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$growth_pars_L.r[[i]]
  shrink_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$shrink_pars_L.r[[i]] 
  frag_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$frag_pars_L.r[[i]]
  surv_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$surv_pars_L.o[[i]] 
  growth_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$growth_pars_L.o[[i]]
  shrink_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$shrink_pars_L.o[[i]]
  frag_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$frag_pars_L.o[[i]]
  
  # run the model
    sim_ij <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars, rest_pars_ij, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)
    
    lab_grow_base[[i]] <- sim_ij
  
}


lab_grow_base_fun <- function(sim_list0, n_sample1, max_yr){
  
  
   r_inf <- 0.056 # inflation rate
      # fixed costs
      c_RS <- 84.6*rest_pars$orchard_size/30 + 1200 # reef stars (cost of reef stars plus cost of boat rental for installation); no inflation because assume this is only an initial cost
      
      # fixed costs that are per year (need to account for inflation)
      # c_perm <- 400*length(2:max_yr)
      c_perm <- sum(400*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # permits for collecting babies (400 per year)
      c_boat <- sum(1200*4*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # boat maintenance (1200 every 3 months/4x per year)
      c_log <- sum(500*12*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # logistics/personnel (500 per month/12x per year)
      
      c_fixed <- c_RS + c_perm + c_boat + c_log
      
      
     # make the holding data frame for growth and survival cases
      
    dt <- data.frame(
  par_rep = rep(NA, n_sample1), # random parameter replicate
  reef_cover_mean = rep(NA, n_sample1), # m2 of coral cover on reef (mean over years 1 to max_yr)
  reef_tiles_out_tot = rep(NA, n_sample1), # total number of tiles outplanted to reef
  reef_area_out_tot = rep(NA, n_sample1), # total area of reef over which tiles were outplanted 
  reef_recruits_out_tot = rep(NA, n_sample1), # total recruits outplanted to reef
  reef_ROI_mean = rep(NA, n_sample1), # average coral cover on reef per dollar spent, averaged over max_yr years
 tot_costs = rep(NA, n_sample1), # total amount spent over length of simulation
 c_fixed = rep(NA, n_sample1), # fixed costs
 c_subs = rep(NA, n_sample1), # costs of substrates
 c_out = rep(NA, n_sample1), # outplanting costs
 c_maint = rep(NA, n_sample1), # orchard maintenance costs
 c_lab = rep(NA, n_sample1), # lab costs
 c_spawn = rep(NA, n_sample1) # costs of collecting spawn
  
)
  
  # calculate the performance metrics for each parameter combination
  for(i in 1:n_sample1){ # for each parameter combination
    
    
  
    
    # fill in the data frame
  
      
      sim_ij <- sim_list0[[i]]
      
      # reef cover
      reef_cover_ij <- model_summ(model_sim = sim_ij, location = "reef", metric = "area_m2", n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments))
 
      dt$par_rep[i] <- i
      dt$reef_cover_mean[i] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dt$reef_cover_sd[i] <- sd(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dt$reef_tiles_out_tot[i] <- sum(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dt$reef_area_out_tot[i] <- dt$reef_tiles_out_tot[i]*5
      dt$reef_recruits_out_tot[i] <- sum(c(sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[2]][2:max_yr], sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[3]][2:max_yr]), na.rm = T)
      
      
      # cost calculations
     
     # variable costs
      # substrate costs
      c_subs <- sum((sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr] + sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[2:max_yr]) *(0.5*1 + 0.5*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))
      
      
    # outplanting and orchard maintenance costs
      c_reef <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # reef
      c_orch <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard
      c_maint <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard maintenance
      
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        #print(ii)
        
 # outplanting costs (assuming you can outplant ~1,250 substrates per day to the reef and 800 per day to the orchard)
        c_reef[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250) + 200*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250 + 380/2000*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]
        
        c_reef[ii-1] <- c_reef[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
       c_orch[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800) + 200*sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800 + 250/2000 * sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]
       
       c_orch[ii-1] <- c_orch[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
        # currently occupied reef stars in the orchard:
        RS_ii <- rep(NA, length(orchard_treatments))
        for(kk in 1:length(orchard_treatments)){ # in case there's more than one orchard
          RS_ii[kk] <- sim_ij$orchard_tiles[[kk]][ii]/30 # 30 tiles per reef star
        }
        
        RS_ii <- sum(RS_ii) # filled reef stars in orchard this year
        
        c_maint[ii-1] <- ifelse(RS_ii == 0, 0, (1.5*530-530)/(2*123-123)*RS_ii + 265) 
        c_maint[ii-1] <- c_maint[ii-1]*2*(1 + r_inf)^(ii) # assume maintenance is performed 2x per year, also add inflation
        
      } # end of loop over 2:max_yr
      
      c_out <- sum(c_reef) + sum(c_orch)
      c_maint <- sum(c_maint)
 
      # lab costs
     # c_lab <- (432*7 + 200*7)*length(2:max_yr) # assume all tiles are only kept in the lab for two weeks
      c_lab0 <- sum((432*7 + 200*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # cost of initial two weeks for all substrates
      c_lab1 <- sum((200*(months_in[j]*30 - 14))*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))# cost of keeping substrates for longer in the lab (substratct the first 2 weeks = 14 days from length of time in lab)
      c_lab <- c_lab0 + c_lab1
    
      
  
      # spawning costs    
      c_spawn <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr))
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- ifelse(sim_ij$reef_babies_used[ii] > 0, 6500, 0) + ifelse(sim_ij$orchard_babies_used[ii] > 0, 3250, 0)
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- c_spawn[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
      }
    
      c_spawn <- sum(c_spawn)
      
      
     tot_costs <- c_fixed + c_subs + c_out + c_maint + c_lab + c_spawn
     
     dt$c_fixed[i] <- c_fixed
     dt$c_subs[i] <- c_subs
     dt$c_out[i] <- c_out
     dt$c_maint[i] <- c_maint
     dt$c_lab[i] <- c_lab
     dt$c_spawn[i] <- c_spawn
     dt$tot_costs[i] <- tot_costs
      
      #dt$tot_dollar_costs[i]
      dt$reef_ROI_mean[i] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr]/tot_costs)
    
      
    } # end of iterations over parameter values
    

  return(dt = dt)
  
}


lab_grow_base5 <- lab_grow_base_fun(sim_list0 = lab_grow_base, n_sample1, max_yr = 6)

lab_grow_base50 <- lab_grow_base_fun(sim_list0 = lab_grow_base, n_sample1, max_yr = 51)


#View(lab_grow_base5)


# repeat with just the mean values?

```



```{r}
# need to iterate over 1) parameter values, 2) post-outplanting survival or growth of recruits kept in lab, and 3) number of weeks to keep in the lab (which matters if there's disturbance and for cost calculations)
#months_in <- seq(from = 0, to = 12, by = 1) # number of months to keep substrates in lab
lab_retain_set <- c(3100/2)

months_in <- 12 # number of months to keep substrates in lab

#L1 <- 10
lab_surv_out <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, length.out = 50) # post-outplanting survival of recruits kept in lab
#lab_surv_out <- c(0.01, 0.1, 0.25)

lab_growth <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, length.out = 50) # proportion of substrates kept in lab that grew into second size class
#lab_growth <- c(0.01, 0.1, 0.25)

lab_growG_list <- list() # holding list for simulations where growth is varied
lab_growS_list <- list() # holding list for simulations where post-outplanting survival is varied

# outer loop = demographic parameter values
# middle loop = number of months to keep in lab
# inner loop = post-outplanting survival or in-lab growth of lab recruits

tic()

for(i in 1:n_sample1){ # for each parameter set
  
  # set up the parameters
  
  surv_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$surv_pars_L.r[[i]] 
  growth_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$growth_pars_L.r[[i]]
  shrink_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$shrink_pars_L.r[[i]] 
  frag_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$frag_pars_L.r[[i]]
  surv_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$surv_pars_L.o[[i]] 
  growth_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$growth_pars_L.o[[i]]
  shrink_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$shrink_pars_L.o[[i]]
  frag_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$frag_pars_L.o[[i]]
  
  
  # holding lists for model simulations with this parameter set
  mod_listG <- list() # growth
  mod_listS <- list() # survival
  
  for(j in 1:length(lab_retain_set)){ # for each lab_retain_max value
    
    rest_pars_ij <- rest_pars
    rest_pars_ij$lab_retain_max <- lab_retain_set[j]
    
    mod_listG2 <- list()
    mod_listS2 <- list()
    
    
    for(k in 1:length(lab_surv_out)){
      
      # survival simulation
      
      # update field survival of the grow-out tiles (smallest size class only for now)
      surv_pars.r_k <- surv_pars.r
      surv_pars.r_k[[1]][[3]][1] <- lab_surv_out[k]

      # run the model
    sim_ij <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r_k, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars, rest_pars_ij, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)
      
      
      # save the output
    mod_listS2[[k]] <- sim_ij
    
    }
    
    for(k in 1:length(lab_growth)){
      
      # growth simulation
    lab_pars_k <- lab_pars
    lab_pars_k$size_props1[2, ] <- c(1-lab_growth[k], lab_growth[k], 0, 0,0)
      
       # run the model
    sim_ij <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars = lab_pars_k, rest_pars_ij, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)
    
    
    # save the output
    mod_listG2[[k]] <- sim_ij
      
    }
   
    
  mod_listG[[j]] <-  mod_listG2 # growth
  mod_listS[[j]] <- mod_listS2 # survival
    
  } # end of iterations over weeks kept in lab
  
  
  lab_growG_list[[i]] <- mod_listG
  lab_growS_list[[i]] <- mod_listS
  
} # end of iterations over parameter sets

toc()


```


```{r}

# function for extracting performance metrics from the lab grow-out simulations
# n_sample1 = number of parameter sets simulated
# lab_retain_set = vector with max number of substrates kept in lab
# lab_surv_out = vector with outplanting survival values that were simulated
# lab_growth = vector with lab growth rates that were simulated
lab_grow_metric_fun <- function(sim_listG, sim_listS, n_sample1, lab_retain_set, lab_surv_out, lab_growth, max_yr){
  
  LS <- length(lab_surv_out) 
  LG <- length(lab_growth) 
  
   r_inf <- 0.056 # inflation rate
      # fixed costs
      c_RS <- 84.6*rest_pars$orchard_size/30 + 1200 # reef stars (cost of reef stars plus cost of boat rental for installation); no inflation because assume this is only an initial cost
      
      # fixed costs that are per year (need to account for inflation)
      # c_perm <- 400*length(2:max_yr)
      c_perm <- sum(400*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # permits for collecting babies (400 per year)
      c_boat <- sum(1200*4*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # boat maintenance (1200 every 3 months/4x per year)
      c_log <- sum(500*12*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # logistics/personnel (500 per month/12x per year)
      
      c_fixed <- c_RS + c_perm + c_boat + c_log
  
  # calculate the performance metrics for each parameter combination
  for(i in 1:n_sample1){ # for each parameter combination
    
    for(j in 1:length(lab_retain_set)){ # for each month kept in the lab
      
      
       # make the holding data frames for growth and survival cases
      
      
    # growth:  
    dtG_i <- data.frame(
  par_rep = rep(i, LG), # random parameter replicate
  lab_retain_max = rep(lab_retain_set[j], LG), # random parameter replicate
  lab_growth = rep(NA, LG), # proportion outplanted to reef
  reef_cover_mean = rep(NA, LG), # m2 of coral cover on reef (mean over years 1 to max_yr)
  reef_tiles_out_tot = rep(NA, LG), # total number of tiles outplanted to reef
  reef_area_out_tot = rep(NA, LG), # total area of reef over which tiles were outplanted 
  reef_recruits_out_tot = rep(NA, LG), # total recruits outplanted to reef
  reef_ROI_mean = rep(NA, LG), # average coral cover on reef per dollar spent, averaged over max_yr years
 tot_costs = rep(NA, LG), # total amount spent over length of simulation
 c_fixed = rep(NA, LG), # fixed costs
 c_subs = rep(NA, LG), # costs of substrates
 c_out = rep(NA, LG), # outplanting costs
 c_maint = rep(NA, LG), # orchard maintenance costs
 c_lab = rep(NA, LG), # lab costs
 c_spawn = rep(NA, LG) # costs of collecting spawn
  
)
    
    # survival data frame
    dtS_i <- data.frame(
  par_rep = rep(i, LS), # random parameter replicate
  lab_retain_max = rep(lab_retain_set[j], LS), # random parameter replicate
  lab_surv_out = rep(NA, LS), # proportion outplanted to reef
  reef_cover_mean = rep(NA, LS), # m2 of coral cover on reef (mean over years 1 to max_yr)
  reef_tiles_out_tot = rep(NA, LS), # total number of tiles outplanted to reef
  reef_area_out_tot = rep(NA, LS), # total area of reef over which tiles were outplanted 
  reef_recruits_out_tot = rep(NA, LS), # total recruits outplanted to reef
  reef_ROI_mean = rep(NA, LS), # average coral cover on reef per dollar spent, averaged over max_yr years
 tot_costs = rep(NA, LS), # total amount spent over length of simulation
 c_fixed = rep(NA, LS), # fixed costs
 c_subs = rep(NA, LS), # costs of substrates
 c_out = rep(NA, LS), # outplanting costs
 c_maint = rep(NA, LS), # orchard maintenance costs
 c_lab = rep(NA, LS), # lab costs
 c_spawn = rep(NA, LS) # costs of collecting spawn
  
)
    
    
    # fill in the data frames
    
     for(k in 1:LG){ # for growth or survival 
      
      sim_ij <- sim_listG[[i]][[j]][[k]]
      
      # reef cover
      reef_cover_ij <- model_summ(model_sim = sim_ij, location = "reef", metric = "area_m2", n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments))
 

      dtG_i$lab_growth[k] <- lab_growth[k]
      dtG_i$reef_cover_mean[k] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtG_i$reef_cover_sd[k] <- sd(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtG_i$reef_tiles_out_tot[k] <- sum(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtG_i$reef_area_out_tot[k] <- dtG_i$reef_tiles_out_tot[k]*5
      dtG_i$reef_recruits_out_tot[k] <- sum(c(sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[2]][2:max_yr], sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[3]][2:max_yr]), na.rm = T)
      
      
      # cost calculations
     
     # variable costs
      # substrate costs
      c_subs <- sum((sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr] + sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[2:max_yr]) *(0.5*1 + 0.5*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))
      
      
    # outplanting and orchard maintenance costs
      c_reef <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # reef
      c_orch <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard
      c_maint <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard maintenance
      
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        #print(ii)
        
 # outplanting costs (assuming you can outplant ~1,250 substrates per day to the reef and 800 per day to the orchard)
        c_reef[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250) + 200*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250 + 380/2000*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]
        
        c_reef[ii-1] <- c_reef[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
       c_orch[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800) + 200*sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800 + 250/2000 * sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]
       
       c_orch[ii-1] <- c_orch[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
        # currently occupied reef stars in the orchard:
        RS_ii <- rep(NA, length(orchard_treatments))
        for(kk in 1:length(orchard_treatments)){ # in case there's more than one orchard
          RS_ii[kk] <- sim_ij$orchard_tiles[[kk]][ii]/30 # 30 tiles per reef star
        }
        
        RS_ii <- sum(RS_ii) # filled reef stars in orchard this year
        
        c_maint[ii-1] <- ifelse(RS_ii == 0, 0, (1.5*530-530)/(2*123-123)*RS_ii + 265) 
        c_maint[ii-1] <- c_maint[ii-1]*2*(1 + r_inf)^(ii) # assume maintenance is performed 2x per year, also add inflation
        
      } # end of loop over 2:max_yr
      
      c_out <- sum(c_reef) + sum(c_orch)
      c_maint <- sum(c_maint)
 
      # lab costs
     # c_lab <- (432*7 + 200*7)*length(2:max_yr) # assume all tiles are only kept in the lab for two weeks
      c_lab0 <- sum((432*7 + 200*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # cost of initial two weeks for all substrates
      c_lab1 <- sum((200*(months_in*30 - 14))*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))# cost of keeping substrates for longer in the lab (substratct the first 2 weeks = 14 days from length of time in lab)
      c_lab <- c_lab0 + c_lab1
    
      
  
      # spawning costs    
      c_spawn <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr))
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- ifelse(sim_ij$reef_babies_used[ii] > 0, 6500, 0) + ifelse(sim_ij$orchard_babies_used[ii] > 0, 3250, 0)
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- c_spawn[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
      }
    
      c_spawn <- sum(c_spawn)
      
      
     tot_costs <- c_fixed + c_subs + c_out + c_maint + c_lab + c_spawn
     
     dtG_i$c_fixed[k] <- c_fixed
     dtG_i$c_subs[k] <- c_subs
     dtG_i$c_out[k] <- c_out
     dtG_i$c_maint[k] <- c_maint
     dtG_i$c_lab[k] <- c_lab
     dtG_i$c_spawn[k] <- c_spawn
     dtG_i$tot_costs[k] <- tot_costs
      
      #dtG_i$tot_dollar_costs[k]
      dtG_i$reef_ROI_mean[k] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr]/tot_costs)
      
    } # end of iteration over growth values
    
    
    # repeat for survival
    
    for(k in 1:LS){ # for growth or survival 
      
      sim_ij <- sim_listS[[i]][[j]][[k]]
      
      # reef cover
      reef_cover_ij <- model_summ(model_sim = sim_ij, location = "reef", metric = "area_m2", n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments))
 

      dtS_i$lab_surv_out[k] <- lab_surv_out[k]
      dtS_i$reef_cover_mean[k] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtS_i$reef_cover_sd[k] <- sd(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtS_i$reef_tiles_out_tot[k] <- sum(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtS_i$reef_area_out_tot[k] <- dtS_i$reef_tiles_out_tot[k]*5
      dtS_i$reef_recruits_out_tot[k] <- sum(c(sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[2]][2:max_yr], sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[3]][2:max_yr]), na.rm = T)
      
      
      # cost calculations
     
     # variable costs
      # substrate costs
      c_subs <- sum((sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr] + sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[2:max_yr]) *(0.5*1 + 0.5*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))
      
      
    # outplanting and orchard maintenance costs
      c_reef <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # reef
      c_orch <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard
      c_maint <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard maintenance
      
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        #print(ii)
        
 # outplanting costs (assuming you can outplant ~1,250 substrates per day to the reef and 800 per day to the orchard)
        c_reef[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250) + 200*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250 + 380/2000*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]
        
        c_reef[ii-1] <- c_reef[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
       c_orch[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800) + 200*sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800 + 250/2000 * sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]
       
       c_orch[ii-1] <- c_orch[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
        # currently occupied reef stars in the orchard:
        RS_ii <- rep(NA, length(orchard_treatments))
        for(kk in 1:length(orchard_treatments)){ # in case there's more than one orchard
          RS_ii[kk] <- sim_ij$orchard_tiles[[kk]][ii]/30 # 30 tiles per reef star
        }
        
        RS_ii <- sum(RS_ii) # filled reef stars in orchard this year
        
        c_maint[ii-1] <- ifelse(RS_ii == 0, 0, (1.5*530-530)/(2*123-123)*RS_ii + 265) 
        c_maint[ii-1] <- c_maint[ii-1]*2*(1 + r_inf)^(ii) # assume maintenance is performed 2x per year, also add inflation
        
      } # end of loop over 2:max_yr
      
      c_out <- sum(c_reef) + sum(c_orch)
      c_maint <- sum(c_maint)
 
      # lab costs
     # c_lab <- (432*7 + 200*7)*length(2:max_yr) # assume all tiles are only kept in the lab for two weeks
      c_lab0 <- sum((432*7 + 200*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # cost of initial two weeks for all substrates
      c_lab1 <- sum((200*(months_in*30 - 14))*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))# cost of keeping substrates for longer in the lab (substratct the first 2 weeks = 14 days from length of time in lab)
      c_lab <- c_lab0 + c_lab1
    
      
  
      # spawning costs    
      c_spawn <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr))
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- ifelse(sim_ij$reef_babies_used[ii] > 0, 6500, 0) + ifelse(sim_ij$orchard_babies_used[ii] > 0, 3250, 0)
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- c_spawn[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
      }
    
      c_spawn <- sum(c_spawn)
      
      
     tot_costs <- c_fixed + c_subs + c_out + c_maint + c_lab + c_spawn
     
     dtS_i$c_fixed[k] <- c_fixed
     dtS_i$c_subs[k] <- c_subs
     dtS_i$c_out[k] <- c_out
     dtS_i$c_maint[k] <- c_maint
     dtS_i$c_lab[k] <- c_lab
     dtS_i$c_spawn[k] <- c_spawn
     dtS_i$tot_costs[k] <- tot_costs
      
      #dtS_i$tot_dollar_costs[k]
      dtS_i$reef_ROI_mean[k] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr]/tot_costs)
      
    } # end of iteration over survival values
    
    
    # store the dataframes
    if(j == 1){
    dtG_j <- dtG_i
    dtS_j <- dtS_i
  } else{
    dtG_j <- rbind(dtG_j, dtG_i)
    dtS_j <- rbind(dtS_j, dtS_i)
  }
    
  }# end of iteration over months kept in lab
    
     # store the full data frames
      if(i == 1){
    dtG <- dtG_j
    dtS <- dtS_j
  } else{
    dtG <- rbind(dtG, dtG_j)
    dtS <- rbind(dtS, dtS_j)
  }
    
    
  
      
    } # end of iterations over parameter values
    

  
  return(list(dtS = dtS, dtG = dtG)) # return the data frames
  
}


# data frame for all the performance metrics at year 5 (use data from time points 2 through 6)
# D0 for no disturbance simulations
lab_grow_5_D0 <- lab_grow_metric_fun(sim_listG = lab_growG_list, sim_listS = lab_growS_list, n_sample1, lab_retain_set, lab_surv_out, lab_growth, max_yr = 6)

# make this a function of the list, n_sample1, L1, and the time point of interest

# data frame for all the performance metrics at year 50 (use data from time points 2 through 51)
lab_grow_50_D0 <- lab_grow_metric_fun(sim_listG = lab_growG_list, sim_listS = lab_growS_list, n_sample1, lab_retain_set, lab_surv_out, lab_growth, max_yr = 51)
```

```{r}
# maybe make plots showing how high survival would need to be to meet different ROI thresholds?


# want to make a data set where for each parameter combination and month, find the min survival value needed to get an ROI of at least ROI_thresh


#dt <- lab_grow_5_D0[[1]]

ROI_thresh_fun <- function(dt, ROI_thresh, lab_retain_set, sim_type){
  
  Lin <- length(lab_retain_set)
  
  for(i in 1:length(unique(dt$par_rep))){
    
    dt_i <- data.frame(
    par_rep = rep(i, Lin),
    lab_retain_max = rep(NA, Lin),
    thresh_val = rep(NA, Lin),
    ROI_val = rep(NA, Lin),
    reef_cover = rep(NA, Lin),
    tot_costs = rep(NA, Lin))
    
    dt_sub0 <- dt %>% filter(par_rep == i)
    
    if(length(ROI_thresh)>1){ # if there is a different threshold value for each parameter value
      ROI_thresh_i <- ROI_thresh[i]
    } else{
      ROI_thresh_i <- ROI_thresh
    }
    
    for(j in 1:Lin){
      
      dt_i$lab_retain_max[j] <- lab_retain_set[j]
      
      dt_sub <- dt_sub0 %>% filter(lab_retain_max == lab_retain_set[j])
      
      if(max(dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean) >= ROI_thresh_i){
        
       rchoice <- min(which(dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean >= ROI_thresh_i))
       
       dt_i$ROI_val[j] <- dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean[rchoice]
       dt_i$reef_cover[j] <- dt_sub$reef_cover_mean[rchoice]
       dt_i$tot_costs[j] <- dt_sub$tot_costs[rchoice]
       
       if(sim_type=="survival"){ # if these simulations varied outplanting survival
         dt_i$thresh_val[j] <- dt_sub$lab_surv_out[rchoice]
         
       }
       
       if(sim_type == "growth"){ # if these simulations varied growth in lab
         dt_i$thresh_val[j] <- dt_sub$lab_growth[rchoice]
       }
       
        
      } 
      
    }
    
    
    if(i == 1){
      dt_out <- dt_i
    } else{
      dt_out <- rbind(dt_out, dt_i)
    }
    
  }
  
  return(dt_out)
  
}

```

```{r}
ROI_surv5 <- ROI_thresh_fun(dt = lab_grow_5_D0$dtS, ROI_thresh, lab_retain_set, sim_type = "survival")

ROI_grow5 <- ROI_thresh_fun(dt = lab_grow_5_D0$dtG, ROI_thresh, lab_retain_set, sim_type = "growth")
```

```{r}

#View(lab_grow_base5)

#View(lab_grow_5_D0$dtS)

dt_plot <- lab_grow_5_D0$dtS
dt_plot0 <- lab_grow_base5

#dt_plot <- lab_grow_50_D0$dtS
#dt_plot0 <- lab_grow_base50

lab_cx <- 0.95

pdf("TempFigs/metrics_growS_5_D0.pdf", height = 7, width = 5)

par(mfrow = c(2, 1))
layout(matrix(c(1, 2), nrow = 2, ncol = 1, byrow = F), widths = rep(1, 1), heights = rep(1, 2))
 #layout.show(2)
par(mar=c(0, 2, 3, 3), oma = c(4.5, 4.5, 2, 0))


# mean reef cover
xlab1 <- NA
ylab1 <- expression("Mean coral cover ("*m^2*")")
plotlab <- "Coral cover on reef"
plot(x = lab_surv_out, y = rep(0, length(lab_surv_out)), type = "l", col = NA, ylim = c(0, max(dt_plot$reef_cover_mean, na.rm = T)), las = 1, xlab = NA, ylab = NA)
mtext(side = 1, xlab1, line = 2.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 2, ylab1, line = 2.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 3, plotlab, adj = 0)
#mtext(side = 3, "5 year simulation, no disturbance", outer = T, line = -1)
for(i in 1:n_sample1){
  dt_sub <- dt_plot[which(dt_plot$par_rep==i),]
  lines(x = dt_sub$lab_surv_out, y = dt_sub$reef_cover_mean, col = adjustcolor(col = "black", alpha.f = 0.1))
}
dt_mean <- dt_plot %>% group_by(lab_surv_out) %>% summarize(mean_val = mean(reef_cover_mean, na.rm = T))
lines(x = dt_mean$lab_surv_out, y = dt_mean$mean_val, col = "black", lwd = 2.5, lty = 2)
dt_mean <- dt_plot0 %>% summarize(mean_val = mean(reef_cover_mean, na.rm = T))
lines(x = lab_surv_out, y = rep(dt_mean$mean_val, length(lab_surv_out)), col = "black", lwd = 2.5, lty = 3)
legend("topleft", legend = c("mean", "reference"), lty = c(2, 3), lwd = 2, bty = "n")


# reef ROI

ylab1 <- expression("Reef ROI ("*m^2~"coral/$)")
plotlab <- "Reef return on investment"
plot(x = lab_surv_out, y = rep(0, length(lab_surv_out)), type = "l", col = NA, ylim = c(0, max(dt_plot$reef_ROI_mean, na.rm = T)), las = 1, xlab = NA, ylab = NA)
mtext(side = 1, xlab1, line = 2.5, outer = T, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 2, ylab1, line = 4, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 3, plotlab, adj = 0)
for(i in 1:n_sample1){
  dt_sub <- dt_plot[which(dt_plot$par_rep==i),]
  lines(x = dt_sub$lab_surv_out, y = dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean, col = adjustcolor(col = "black", alpha.f = 0.1))
}
dt_mean <- dt_plot %>% group_by(lab_surv_out) %>% summarize(mean_val = mean(reef_ROI_mean, na.rm = T))
lines(x = dt_mean$lab_surv_out, y = dt_mean$mean_val, col = "black", lwd = 2.5, lty = 2)
dt_mean <- dt_plot0 %>% summarize(mean_val = mean(reef_ROI_mean, na.rm = T))
lines(x = lab_surv_out, y = rep(dt_mean$mean_val, length(lab_surv_out)), col = "black", lwd = 2.5, lty = 3)


dev.off()


# repeat for growth

 #layout.show(2)

# mean reef cover

ylab1 <- expression("Mean coral cover ("*m^2*")")
plotlab <- "Coral cover on reef"
plot(x = lab_surv_out, y = rep(0, length(lab_surv_out)), type = "l", col = NA, ylim = c(0, max(dt_plot$reef_cover_mean, na.rm = T)), las = 1, xlab = NA, ylab = NA)
mtext(side = 1, xlab1, line = 2.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 2, ylab1, line = 2.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 3, plotlab, adj = 0)
#mtext(side = 3, "5 year simulation, no disturbance", outer = T, line = -1)

}
```
