```{r}
dt_plot0 <- lab_grow_base5 %>% filter(reef_cover_mean > 0)
dt_plot <- lab_grow_5_D0$dtS %>% filter(par_rep %in% dt_plot0$par_rep)

#dt_plot0 <- lab_grow_base50 %>% filter(reef_cover_mean > 0)
#dt_plot <- lab_grow_50_D0$dtS %>% filter(par_rep %in% dt_plot0$par_rep)


# relative to defaults
xlab1 <- NA
ylab1 <- "x default"#expression("Mean coral cover ("*m^2*")")
plotlab <- "Relative coral cover on reef"
plot(x = lab_surv_out, y = rep(0, length(lab_surv_out)), type = "l", col = NA, ylim = c(0, 10), las = 1, xlab = NA, ylab = NA)
mtext(side = 1, xlab1, line = 2.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 2, ylab1, line = 2.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 3, plotlab, adj = 0)
mtext(side = 3, "5 year simulation, no disturbance", outer = T, line = -1)
for(i in unique(dt_plot$par_rep)){
  dt_sub <- dt_plot[which(dt_plot$par_rep==i),]
  dt_sub0 <- dt_plot0[which(dt_plot0$par_rep==i),]
  lines(x = dt_sub$lab_surv_out, y = dt_sub$reef_cover_mean/dt_sub0$reef_cover_mean, col = adjustcolor(col = "black", alpha.f = 0.2))
}
abline(h = 1, lty = 2)


# reef ROI
xlab1 <- "Post-outplanting survival of retained substrates"
ylab1 <- "x default" #expression("Reef ROI ("*m^2~"coral/$)")
plotlab <- "Relative reef return on investment"
plot(x = lab_surv_out, y = rep(0, length(lab_surv_out)), type = "l", col = NA, ylim = c(0, 6), las = 1, xlab = NA, ylab = NA)
mtext(side = 1, xlab1, line = 2.5, outer = T, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 2, ylab1, line = 3.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 3, plotlab, adj = 0)
for(i in unique(dt_plot$par_rep)){
  dt_sub <- dt_plot[which(dt_plot$par_rep==i),]
  dt_sub0 <- dt_plot0[which(dt_plot0$par_rep==i),]
  lines(x = dt_sub$lab_surv_out, y = dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean/dt_sub0$reef_ROI_mean, col = adjustcolor(col = "black", alpha.f = 0.2))
}
abline(h = 1, lty = 2)



# repeat for growth case
dt_plot0 <- lab_grow_base5 %>% filter(reef_cover_mean > 0)
dt_plot <- lab_grow_5_D0$dtG %>% filter(par_rep %in% dt_plot0$par_rep)

# relative to defaults
xlab1 <- NA
ylab1 <- "x default"#expression("Mean coral cover ("*m^2*")")
plotlab <- "Relative coral cover on reef"
plot(x = lab_growth, y = rep(0, length(lab_growth)), type = "l", col = NA, ylim = c(0, 50), las = 1, xlab = NA, ylab = NA)
mtext(side = 1, xlab1, line = 2.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 2, ylab1, line = 2.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 3, plotlab, adj = 0)
mtext(side = 3, "5 year simulation, no disturbance", outer = T, line = -1)
for(i in unique(dt_plot$par_rep)){
  dt_sub <- dt_plot[which(dt_plot$par_rep==i),]
  dt_sub0 <- dt_plot0[which(dt_plot0$par_rep==i),]
  lines(x = dt_sub$lab_growth, y = dt_sub$reef_cover_mean/dt_sub0$reef_cover_mean, col = adjustcolor(col = "black", alpha.f = 0.2))
}
abline(h = 1, lty = 2)


# reef ROI
xlab1 <- "Post-outplanting survival of retained substrates"
ylab1 <- "x default" #expression("Reef ROI ("*m^2~"coral/$)")
plotlab <- "Relative reef return on investment"
plot(x = lab_growth, y = rep(0, length(lab_growth)), type = "l", col = NA, ylim = c(0, 30), las = 1, xlab = NA, ylab = NA)
mtext(side = 1, xlab1, line = 2.5, outer = T, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 2, ylab1, line = 3.5, cex = lab_cx)
mtext(side = 3, plotlab, adj = 0)
for(i in unique(dt_plot$par_rep)){
  dt_sub <- dt_plot[which(dt_plot$par_rep==i),]
  dt_sub0 <- dt_plot0[which(dt_plot0$par_rep==i),]
  lines(x = dt_sub$lab_growth, y = dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean/dt_sub0$reef_ROI_mean, col = adjustcolor(col = "black", alpha.f = 0.2))
}
abline(h = 1, lty = 2)


#dev.off()


```


make plots where x-axis is number of tiles kept in the lab and y-axis is min survival needed for different ROI thresholds? With lines connecting parameter combinations to show when the thresholds become NA?


```{r}
# need to iterate over 1) parameter values, 2) post-outplanting survival or growth of recruits kept in lab, and 3) number of weeks to keep in the lab (which matters if there's disturbance and for cost calculations)
#months_in <- seq(from = 0, to = 12, by = 1) # number of months to keep substrates in lab
#lab_retain_set <- c(3100/2)
#lab_retain_set <- seq(from = 0, to = 3100, by = 3100/4)
lab_retain_set <- seq(from = 3100/4, to = 3100, by = 3100/4)

months_in <- 12 # number of months to keep substrates in lab

#L1 <- 10
lab_surv_out <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, length.out = 50) # post-outplanting survival of recruits kept in lab
#lab_surv_out <- c(0.01, 0.1, 0.25)

lab_growth <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, length.out = 50) # proportion of substrates kept in lab that grew into second size class
#lab_growth <- c(0.01, 0.1, 0.25)

lab_growG_listL <- list() # holding list for simulations where growth is varied
lab_growS_listL <- list() # holding list for simulations where post-outplanting survival is varied

# outer loop = demographic parameter values
# middle loop = number of months to keep in lab
# inner loop = post-outplanting survival or in-lab growth of lab recruits

tic()

for(i in 1:n_sample1){ # for each parameter set
  
  # set up the parameters
  
  surv_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$surv_pars_L.r[[i]] 
  growth_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$growth_pars_L.r[[i]]
  shrink_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$shrink_pars_L.r[[i]] 
  frag_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$frag_pars_L.r[[i]]
  surv_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$surv_pars_L.o[[i]] 
  growth_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$growth_pars_L.o[[i]]
  shrink_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$shrink_pars_L.o[[i]]
  frag_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$frag_pars_L.o[[i]]
  
  
  # holding lists for model simulations with this parameter set
  mod_listG <- list() # growth
  mod_listS <- list() # survival
  
  for(j in 1:length(lab_retain_set)){ # for each lab_retain_max value
    
    rest_pars_ij <- rest_pars
    rest_pars_ij$lab_retain_max <- lab_retain_set[j]
    
    mod_listG2 <- list()
    mod_listS2 <- list()
    
    
    for(k in 1:length(lab_surv_out)){
      
      # survival simulation
      
      # update field survival of the grow-out tiles (smallest size class only for now)
      surv_pars.r_k <- surv_pars.r
      surv_pars.r_k[[1]][[3]][1] <- lab_surv_out[k]

      # run the model
    sim_ij <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r_k, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars, rest_pars_ij, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)
      
      
      # save the output
    mod_listS2[[k]] <- sim_ij
    
    }
    
    for(k in 1:length(lab_growth)){
      
      # growth simulation
    lab_pars_k <- lab_pars
    lab_pars_k$size_props1[2, ] <- c(1-lab_growth[k], lab_growth[k], 0, 0,0)
      
       # run the model
    sim_ij <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars = lab_pars_k, rest_pars_ij, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)
    
    
    # save the output
    mod_listG2[[k]] <- sim_ij
      
    }
   
    
  mod_listG[[j]] <-  mod_listG2 # growth
  mod_listS[[j]] <- mod_listS2 # survival
    
  } # end of iterations over weeks kept in lab
  
  
  lab_growG_listL[[i]] <- mod_listG
  lab_growS_listL[[i]] <- mod_listS
  
} # end of iterations over parameter sets

toc()


```


```{r}
# process the results
lab_grow_5_D0L <- lab_grow_metric_fun(sim_listG = lab_growG_listL, sim_listS = lab_growS_listL, n_sample1, lab_retain_set, lab_surv_out, lab_growth, max_yr = 6)


```


396.995 sec elapsed


```{r}
# calculate the threshold values


#ROI_thresh <- 0.0001


#View(lab_grow_base5)
ROI_thresh <-lab_grow_base5$reef_ROI_mean

#hist(lab_grow_5_D0[[1]]$reef_ROI_mean)
#hist(lab_grow_5_D0[[2]]$reef_ROI_mean)


ROI_surv5_base <- ROI_thresh_fun(dt = lab_grow_5_D0L$dtS, ROI_thresh = lab_grow_base5$reef_ROI_mean, lab_retain_set, sim_type = "survival") %>% filter(lab_retain_max > 0)

ROI_grow5_base <- ROI_thresh_fun(dt = lab_grow_5_D0L$dtG, ROI_thresh, lab_retain_set, sim_type = "growth") %>% filter(lab_retain_max > 0)



# repeat with a higher threshold
# ROI of 0.0001m2/$: 0.0001x10000 = 1 m2 per $10000 spent

ROI_surv5_0001 <- ROI_thresh_fun(dt = lab_grow_5_D0L$dtS, ROI_thresh = 0.0001, lab_retain_set, sim_type = "survival") %>% filter(lab_retain_max > 0)

ROI_grow5_0001 <- ROI_thresh_fun(dt = lab_grow_5_D0L$dtG, ROI_thresh = 0.0001, lab_retain_set, sim_type = "growth") %>% filter(lab_retain_max > 0)
```

```{r}
}

for(i in 1:n_sample1){
  
  dt_sub <- dt_plot %>% filter(par_rep==i)
  lines(x = lab_retain_set, y = dt_sub$thresh_val, type = "l", col = adjustcolor("black", alpha.f = 0.3))
}



dt_plot <- ROI_grow5_base

plot(x = lab_retain_set, y = lab_retain_set, col = NA, ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, xaxt = "n")
axis(side = 1, at = lab_retain_set)
mtext(side = 2, "Min. in-lab \ngrowth needed", line = 2.5)
for(i in 1:n_sample1){
  
  dt_sub <- dt_plot %>% filter(par_rep==i)
  lines(x = lab_retain_set, y = dt_sub$thresh_val, type = "l", col = adjustcolor("black", alpha.f = 0.3))
}



dt_plot <- ROI_grow5_0001

plot(x = lab_retain_set, y = lab_retain_set, col = NA, ylim = c(0, 1), las = 1, xaxt = "n", yaxt = "n")
axis(side = 1, at = lab_retain_set)
axis(side = 2, at = seq(from = 0, to = 1, by = 0.2), labels = NA)
mtext(side = 1, "Substrates retained in lab", outer = T, line = 2.5)
for(i in 1:n_sample1){
  
  dt_sub <- dt_plot %>% filter(par_rep==i)
  lines(x = lab_retain_set, y = dt_sub$thresh_val, type = "l", col = adjustcolor("black", alpha.f = 0.3))
}


dev.off()
```

```{r}
# need to iterate over 1) parameter values, 2) post-outplanting survival or growth of recruits kept in lab, and 3) number of weeks to keep in the lab (which matters if there's disturbance and for cost calculations)
#months_in <- seq(from = 0, to = 12, by = 1) # number of months to keep substrates in lab
months_in <- c(4, 8, 12)

#L1 <- 10
lab_surv_out <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, length.out = 50) # post-outplanting survival of recruits kept in lab
#lab_surv_out <- c(0.01, 0.1, 0.25)

lab_growth <- seq(from = 0, to = 1, length.out = 50) # proportion of substrates kept in lab that grew into second size class
#lab_growth <- c(0.01, 0.1, 0.25)

lab_growG_list <- list() # holding list for simulations where growth is varied
lab_growS_list <- list() # holding list for simulations where post-outplanting survival is varied

# outer loop = demographic parameter values
# middle loop = number of months to keep in lab
# inner loop = post-outplanting survival or in-lab growth of lab recruits

tic()

for(i in 1:n_sample1){ # for each parameter set
  
  # set up the parameters
  
  surv_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$surv_pars_L.r[[i]] 
  growth_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$growth_pars_L.r[[i]]
  shrink_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$shrink_pars_L.r[[i]] 
  frag_pars.r <- all_pars_R2$frag_pars_L.r[[i]]
  surv_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$surv_pars_L.o[[i]] 
  growth_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$growth_pars_L.o[[i]]
  shrink_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$shrink_pars_L.o[[i]]
  frag_pars.o <- all_pars_R2$frag_pars_L.o[[i]]
  
  
  # holding lists for model simulations with this parameter set
  mod_listG <- list() # growth
  mod_listS <- list() # survival
  
  for(j in 1:length(months_in)){ # for each proportion outplanted to reef
    
    mod_listG2 <- list()
    mod_listS2 <- list()
    
    
    for(k in 1:length(lab_surv_out)){
      
      # survival simulation
      
      # update field survival of the grow-out tiles (smallest size class only for now)
      surv_pars.r_k <- surv_pars.r
      surv_pars.r_k[[1]][[3]][1] <- lab_surv_out[k]

      # run the model
    sim_ij <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r_k, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars, rest_pars, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)
      
      
      # save the output
    mod_listS2[[k]] <- sim_ij
    
    }
    
    for(k in 1:length(lab_growth)){
      
      # growth simulation
    lab_pars_k <- lab_pars
    lab_pars_k$size_props1[2, ] <- c(1-lab_growth[k], lab_growth[k], 0, 0,0)
      
       # run the model
    sim_ij <- rse_mod1(years, n, A_mids, surv_pars.r, dens_pars.r, growth_pars.r, shrink_pars.r, frag_pars.r, fec_pars.r, surv_pars.o, dens_pars.o, growth_pars.o, shrink_pars.o, frag_pars.o, fec_pars.o, lambda, lambda_R, sigma_s, sigma_f, ext_rand, seeds, dist_yrs, dist_pars.r, dist_effects.r, dist_pars.o, dist_effects.o, orchard_treatments, reef_treatments, lab_treatments, lab_pars = lab_pars_k, rest_pars, N0.r, N0.o, N0.l)
    
    
    # save the output
    mod_listG2[[k]] <- sim_ij
      
    }
   
    
  mod_listG[[j]] <-  mod_listG2 # growth
  mod_listS[[j]] <- mod_listS2 # survival
    
  } # end of iterations over weeks kept in lab
  
  
  lab_growG_list[[i]] <- mod_listG
  lab_growS_list[[i]] <- mod_listS
  
} # end of iterations over parameter sets

toc()


```


```{r}

# function for extracting performance metrics from the lab grow-out simulations
# n_sample1 = number of parameter sets simulated
# months_in = vector with months kept in lab that were simulated
# lab_surv_out = vector with outplanting survival values that were simulated
# lab_growth = vector with lab growth rates that were simulated
lab_grow_metric_fun <- function(sim_listG, sim_listS, n_sample1, months_in, lab_surv_out, lab_growth, max_yr){
  
  LS <- length(lab_surv_out) 
  LG <- length(lab_growth) 
  
   r_inf <- 0.056 # inflation rate
      # fixed costs
      c_RS <- 84.6*rest_pars$orchard_size/30 + 1200 # reef stars (cost of reef stars plus cost of boat rental for installation); no inflation because assume this is only an initial cost
      
      # fixed costs that are per year (need to account for inflation)
      # c_perm <- 400*length(2:max_yr)
      c_perm <- sum(400*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # permits for collecting babies (400 per year)
      c_boat <- sum(1200*4*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # boat maintenance (1200 every 3 months/4x per year)
      c_log <- sum(500*12*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # logistics/personnel (500 per month/12x per year)
      
      c_fixed <- c_RS + c_perm + c_boat + c_log
  
  # calculate the performance metrics for each parameter combination
  for(i in 1:n_sample1){ # for each parameter combination
    
    for(j in 1:length(months_in)){ # for each month kept in the lab
      
      
       # make the holding data frames for growth and survival cases
      
      
    # growth:  
    dtG_i <- data.frame(
  par_rep = rep(i, LG), # random parameter replicate
  lab_months = rep(months_in[j], LG), # random parameter replicate
  lab_growth = rep(NA, LG), # proportion outplanted to reef
  reef_cover_mean = rep(NA, LG), # m2 of coral cover on reef (mean over years 1 to max_yr)
  reef_tiles_out_tot = rep(NA, LG), # total number of tiles outplanted to reef
  reef_area_out_tot = rep(NA, LG), # total area of reef over which tiles were outplanted 
  reef_recruits_out_tot = rep(NA, LG), # total recruits outplanted to reef
  reef_ROI_mean = rep(NA, LG), # average coral cover on reef per dollar spent, averaged over max_yr years
 tot_costs = rep(NA, LG), # total amount spent over length of simulation
 c_fixed = rep(NA, LG), # fixed costs
 c_subs = rep(NA, LG), # costs of substrates
 c_out = rep(NA, LG), # outplanting costs
 c_maint = rep(NA, LG), # orchard maintenance costs
 c_lab = rep(NA, LG), # lab costs
 c_spawn = rep(NA, LG) # costs of collecting spawn
  
)
    
    # survival data frame
    dtS_i <- data.frame(
  par_rep = rep(i, LS), # random parameter replicate
  lab_months = rep(months_in[j], LS), # random parameter replicate
  lab_surv_out = rep(NA, LS), # proportion outplanted to reef
  reef_cover_mean = rep(NA, LS), # m2 of coral cover on reef (mean over years 1 to max_yr)
  reef_tiles_out_tot = rep(NA, LS), # total number of tiles outplanted to reef
  reef_area_out_tot = rep(NA, LS), # total area of reef over which tiles were outplanted 
  reef_recruits_out_tot = rep(NA, LS), # total recruits outplanted to reef
  reef_ROI_mean = rep(NA, LS), # average coral cover on reef per dollar spent, averaged over max_yr years
 tot_costs = rep(NA, LS), # total amount spent over length of simulation
 c_fixed = rep(NA, LS), # fixed costs
 c_subs = rep(NA, LS), # costs of substrates
 c_out = rep(NA, LS), # outplanting costs
 c_maint = rep(NA, LS), # orchard maintenance costs
 c_lab = rep(NA, LS), # lab costs
 c_spawn = rep(NA, LS) # costs of collecting spawn
  
)
    
    
    # fill in the data frames
    
     for(k in 1:LG){ # for growth or survival 
      
      sim_ij <- sim_listG[[i]][[j]][[k]]
      
      # reef cover
      reef_cover_ij <- model_summ(model_sim = sim_ij, location = "reef", metric = "area_m2", n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments))
 

      dtG_i$lab_growth[k] <- lab_growth[k]
      dtG_i$reef_cover_mean[k] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtG_i$reef_cover_sd[k] <- sd(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtG_i$reef_tiles_out_tot[k] <- sum(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtG_i$reef_area_out_tot[k] <- dtG_i$reef_tiles_out_tot[k]*5
      dtG_i$reef_recruits_out_tot[k] <- sum(c(sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[2]][2:max_yr], sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[3]][2:max_yr]), na.rm = T)
      
      
      # cost calculations
     
     # variable costs
      # substrate costs
      c_subs <- sum((sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr] + sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[2:max_yr]) *(0.5*1 + 0.5*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))
      
      
    # outplanting and orchard maintenance costs
      c_reef <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # reef
      c_orch <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard
      c_maint <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard maintenance
      
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        #print(ii)
        
 # outplanting costs (assuming you can outplant ~1,250 substrates per day to the reef and 800 per day to the orchard)
        c_reef[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250) + 200*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250 + 380/2000*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]
        
        c_reef[ii-1] <- c_reef[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
       c_orch[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800) + 200*sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800 + 250/2000 * sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]
       
       c_orch[ii-1] <- c_orch[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
        # currently occupied reef stars in the orchard:
        RS_ii <- rep(NA, length(orchard_treatments))
        for(kk in 1:length(orchard_treatments)){ # in case there's more than one orchard
          RS_ii[kk] <- sim_ij$orchard_tiles[[kk]][ii]/30 # 30 tiles per reef star
        }
        
        RS_ii <- sum(RS_ii) # filled reef stars in orchard this year
        
        c_maint[ii-1] <- ifelse(RS_ii == 0, 0, (1.5*530-530)/(2*123-123)*RS_ii + 265) 
        c_maint[ii-1] <- c_maint[ii-1]*2*(1 + r_inf)^(ii) # assume maintenance is performed 2x per year, also add inflation
        
      } # end of loop over 2:max_yr
      
      c_out <- sum(c_reef) + sum(c_orch)
      c_maint <- sum(c_maint)
 
      # lab costs
     # c_lab <- (432*7 + 200*7)*length(2:max_yr) # assume all tiles are only kept in the lab for two weeks
      c_lab0 <- sum((432*7 + 200*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # cost of initial two weeks for all substrates
      c_lab1 <- sum((200*(months_in[j]*30 - 14))*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))# cost of keeping substrates for longer in the lab (substratct the first 2 weeks = 14 days from length of time in lab)
      c_lab <- c_lab0 + c_lab1
    
      
  
      # spawning costs    
      c_spawn <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr))
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- ifelse(sim_ij$reef_babies_used[ii] > 0, 6500, 0) + ifelse(sim_ij$orchard_babies_used[ii] > 0, 3250, 0)
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- c_spawn[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
      }
    
      c_spawn <- sum(c_spawn)
      
      
     tot_costs <- c_fixed + c_subs + c_out + c_maint + c_lab + c_spawn
     
     dtG_i$c_fixed[k] <- c_fixed
     dtG_i$c_subs[k] <- c_subs
     dtG_i$c_out[k] <- c_out
     dtG_i$c_maint[k] <- c_maint
     dtG_i$c_lab[k] <- c_lab
     dtG_i$c_spawn[k] <- c_spawn
     dtG_i$tot_costs[k] <- tot_costs
      
      #dtG_i$tot_dollar_costs[k]
      dtG_i$reef_ROI_mean[k] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr]/tot_costs)
      
    } # end of iteration over growth values
    
    
    # repeat for survival
    
    for(k in 1:LS){ # for growth or survival 
      
      sim_ij <- sim_listS[[i]][[j]][[k]]
      
      # reef cover
      reef_cover_ij <- model_summ(model_sim = sim_ij, location = "reef", metric = "area_m2", n_reef = length(reef_treatments), n_orchard = length(orchard_treatments), n_lab = length(lab_treatments))
 

      dtS_i$lab_surv_out[k] <- lab_surv_out[k]
      dtS_i$reef_cover_mean[k] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtS_i$reef_cover_sd[k] <- sd(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtS_i$reef_tiles_out_tot[k] <- sum(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr], na.rm = T)
      dtS_i$reef_area_out_tot[k] <- dtS_i$reef_tiles_out_tot[k]*5
      dtS_i$reef_recruits_out_tot[k] <- sum(c(sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[2]][2:max_yr], sim_ij$reef_out[[1]][[3]][2:max_yr]), na.rm = T)
      
      
      # cost calculations
     
     # variable costs
      # substrate costs
      c_subs <- sum((sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[2:max_yr] + sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[2:max_yr]) *(0.5*1 + 0.5*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))
      
      
    # outplanting and orchard maintenance costs
      c_reef <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # reef
      c_orch <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard
      c_maint <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr)) # orchard maintenance
      
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        #print(ii)
        
 # outplanting costs (assuming you can outplant ~1,250 substrates per day to the reef and 800 per day to the orchard)
        c_reef[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250) + 200*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]/1250 + 380/2000*sim_ij$reef_tiles_out[ii]
        
        c_reef[ii-1] <- c_reef[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
       c_orch[ii-1] <- 300*ceiling(sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800) + 200*sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]/800 + 250/2000 * sim_ij$orchard_tiles_out[ii]
       
       c_orch[ii-1] <- c_orch[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
        # currently occupied reef stars in the orchard:
        RS_ii <- rep(NA, length(orchard_treatments))
        for(kk in 1:length(orchard_treatments)){ # in case there's more than one orchard
          RS_ii[kk] <- sim_ij$orchard_tiles[[kk]][ii]/30 # 30 tiles per reef star
        }
        
        RS_ii <- sum(RS_ii) # filled reef stars in orchard this year
        
        c_maint[ii-1] <- ifelse(RS_ii == 0, 0, (1.5*530-530)/(2*123-123)*RS_ii + 265) 
        c_maint[ii-1] <- c_maint[ii-1]*2*(1 + r_inf)^(ii) # assume maintenance is performed 2x per year, also add inflation
        
      } # end of loop over 2:max_yr
      
      c_out <- sum(c_reef) + sum(c_orch)
      c_maint <- sum(c_maint)
 
      # lab costs
     # c_lab <- (432*7 + 200*7)*length(2:max_yr) # assume all tiles are only kept in the lab for two weeks
      c_lab0 <- sum((432*7 + 200*7)*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr)) # cost of initial two weeks for all substrates
      c_lab1 <- sum((200*(months_in[j]*30 - 14))*(1 + r_inf)^(2:max_yr))# cost of keeping substrates for longer in the lab (substratct the first 2 weeks = 14 days from length of time in lab)
      c_lab <- c_lab0 + c_lab1
    
      
  
      # spawning costs    
      c_spawn <- rep(NA, length(2:max_yr))
      for(ii in 2:max_yr){
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- ifelse(sim_ij$reef_babies_used[ii] > 0, 6500, 0) + ifelse(sim_ij$orchard_babies_used[ii] > 0, 3250, 0)
        c_spawn[ii-1] <- c_spawn[ii-1]*(1 + r_inf)^(ii)
        
      }
    
      c_spawn <- sum(c_spawn)
      
      
     tot_costs <- c_fixed + c_subs + c_out + c_maint + c_lab + c_spawn
     
     dtS_i$c_fixed[k] <- c_fixed
     dtS_i$c_subs[k] <- c_subs
     dtS_i$c_out[k] <- c_out
     dtS_i$c_maint[k] <- c_maint
     dtS_i$c_lab[k] <- c_lab
     dtS_i$c_spawn[k] <- c_spawn
     dtS_i$tot_costs[k] <- tot_costs
      
      #dtS_i$tot_dollar_costs[k]
      dtS_i$reef_ROI_mean[k] <- mean(reef_cover_ij[2:max_yr]/tot_costs)
      
    } # end of iteration over survival values
    
    
    # store the dataframes
    if(j == 1){
    dtG_j <- dtG_i
    dtS_j <- dtS_i
  } else{
    dtG_j <- rbind(dtG_j, dtG_i)
    dtS_j <- rbind(dtS_j, dtS_i)
  }
    
  }# end of iteration over months kept in lab
    
     # store the full data frames
      if(i == 1){
    dtG <- dtG_j
    dtS <- dtS_j
  } else{
    dtG <- rbind(dtG, dtG_j)
    dtS <- rbind(dtS, dtS_j)
  }
    
    
  
      
    } # end of iterations over parameter values
    

  
  return(list(dtS = dtS, dtG = dtG)) # return the data frames
  
}


# data frame for all the performance metrics at year 5 (use data from time points 2 through 6)
# D0 for no disturbance simulations
lab_grow_5_D0 <- lab_grow_metric_fun(sim_listG = lab_growG_list, sim_listS = lab_growS_list, n_sample1, months_in, lab_surv_out, lab_growth, max_yr = 6)

# make this a function of the list, n_sample1, L1, and the time point of interest

# data frame for all the performance metrics at year 50 (use data from time points 2 through 51)
lab_grow_50_D0 <- lab_grow_metric_fun(sim_listG = lab_growG_list, sim_listS = lab_growS_list, n_sample1, months_in, lab_surv_out, lab_growth, max_yr = 51)
```

```{r}
# maybe make plots showing how high survival would need to be to meet different ROI thresholds?


# want to make a data set where for each parameter combination and month, find the min survival value needed to get an ROI of at least ROI_thresh


#dt <- lab_grow_5_D0[[1]]

ROI_thresh_fun <- function(dt, ROI_thresh, months_in, sim_type){
  
  Lin <- length(months_in)
  
  for(i in 1:length(unique(dt$par_rep))){
    
    dt_i <- data.frame(
    par_rep = rep(i, Lin),
    lab_months = rep(NA, Lin),
    thresh_val = rep(NA, Lin),
    ROI_val = rep(NA, Lin),
    reef_cover = rep(NA, Lin),
    tot_costs = rep(NA, Lin))
    
    dt_sub0 <- dt %>% filter(par_rep == i)
    
    for(j in 1:Lin){
      
      dt_i$lab_months[j] <- months_in[j]
      
      dt_sub <- dt_sub0 %>% filter(lab_months == months_in[j])
      
      if(max(dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean) >= ROI_thresh){
        
       rchoice <- min(which(dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean >= ROI_thresh))
       
       dt_i$ROI_val[j] <- dt_sub$reef_ROI_mean[rchoice]
       dt_i$reef_cover[j] <- dt_sub$reef_cover_mean[rchoice]
       dt_i$tot_costs[j] <- dt_sub$tot_costs[rchoice]
       
       if(sim_type=="survival"){ # if these simulations varied outplanting survival
         dt_i$thresh_val[j] <- dt_sub$lab_surv_out[rchoice]
         
       }
       
       if(sim_type == "growth"){ # if these simulations varied growth in lab
         dt_i$thresh_val[j] <- dt_sub$lab_growth[rchoice]
       }
       
        
      } 
      
    }
    
    
    if(i == 1){
      dt_out <- dt_i
    } else{
      dt_out <- rbind(dt_out, dt_i)
    }
    
  }
  
  return(dt_out)
  
}

```

```{r}
ROI_surv5 <- ROI_thresh_fun(dt = lab_grow_5_D0$dtS, ROI_thresh, months_in, sim_type = "survival")

ROI_grow5 <- ROI_thresh_fun(dt = lab_grow_5_D0$dtG, ROI_thresh, months_in, sim_type = "growth")
```

should change this to vary length of time the tiles are kept in the lab (from a few months to a year) and then play with assumptions about how survival/growth scale with length of time kept in the lab. Assume fixed proportions of substrates are retained, e.g., 50%

maybe the question is how much survival and/or growth needs to increase relative to corals outplanted immediately (growth could be in the lab, i.e, proportion that are in size class 1 or 2 when outplanted) to make it "worth it" to keep the substrates in the lab for a certain amount of time? By "worth it", maybe that's a higher ROI than if everything was outplanted immediately?
-- BUT also have the question of how long they should be kept in the lab... so could either approach this as 1) have some relationship btw weeks in lab and growth/survival, and for each determine how many weeks to keep them, or 2) for each week kept in lab, determine how big the increase in growth and/or survival would need to be to make it worth it to keep them for that many weeks. Maybe do the latter since there are lots of different possible relationships


so the main question of this scenario is how long to keep substrates in the lab to increase total reef cover and reef ROI (really how much survival of outplants needs to increase or how much growth needs to happen at x weeks to make it worth it to keep them in the lab for x weeks), and explore this as a function of lab and field disturbance regimes


Simplified plot = number of weeks kept in lab on x-axis, y-axis = min survival or growth needed for increase in ROI on y-axis
